In this case, the workflow administrator can still run the workflow or delete the step, but cannot view the connector configuration, modify the step, or change the connection. Example:![Example of a step that cannot be edited.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/connection_error_example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU2MzYsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.CcZ8W7Z8G98RTZaxZckYWPH9Jf5PAaknhzg2FyQOyYM&_LANG=enus "Example of a step that cannot be edited.")For more information, see [Access and Permissions for Extension App Connections](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=cond7058d74-97dd-4ec3-9a23-86978310f74b.html&_LANG=enus "Outlines the access levels and permissions required to edit or publish workflow steps that use private or shared extension application connections.").

Process builder
:   The process builder is a Docusign user who creates, edits, and
    publishes their own workflows. They can also use any published workflow to start
    an agreement process. The builder role requires access to the Docusign applications and supporting features they will use to
    build workflow steps. They will authorize these applications to use their
    account to run workflow steps.
:   Required user permissions:

    - Workflows: Create
    - Allow user to send envelopes: enabled
    - Web Forms: Create
    - Templates: Create

Preparer
:   The preparer runs published workflows to start the agreement process and
    monitors the progress of running instances. The preparer has access to use any
    published workflow on the account to start an agreement process. The preparer
    needs only limited permissions as the agreement process gets required
    authorization and permissions from the process builder's account.
:   Required user permissions:

    - Workflows: Prepare

title: Workflow Builder Roles and Permission Requirements
heading_path: Workflow Builder Roles and Permission Requirements
text:
needs only limited permissions as the agreement process gets required
    authorization and permissions from the process builder's account.
:   Required user permissions:

    - Workflows: Prepare

    Note: See the topic [Configure a DS Sender Profile for Workflow Builder Preparer Permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=tsk1906294d-5fb2-4f24-8764-7b59448b20f3.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to configure a custom Docusign DS Sender permission profile to grant users Workflow Builder preparer access.") for how to add a user profile for the Preparer role.

Participant
:   Participants represent the end users who will participate in an agreement
    workflow. Participants do not need a Docusign account to receive and sign documents. However, if they do have an account, any envelopes generated in a workflow for their user credentials will register in that account.
:   Required user permissions: N/A – no Docusign account required.

title: How to Start a Workflow
text:
Learn how the different start methods determine how to start a workflow.

Published workflows have a defined start method. Depending on which start method is applied,
the workflow is initiated in different ways. For all methods, the workflow must be
published to start an instance.

Once a workflow instance is started, it has 120 days to complete. If the instance is still in process after 120 days, it will expire.

Note: Paused workflows cannot generate new instances, regardless of the
start method. The workflow status must be "Published" to start an instance.

From a Link
:   Once published, a workflow with a link start has a URL. Use the URL to embed it on your
    website or share directly with the participant who will start a workflow
    instance. Select the link icon on the Workflows list
    page to copy the URL. ![Screenshot of the Workflows list showing the link icon for copying the URL.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/workflowlink.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU5MTAsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.JQx5-_ZNbAKDXjnBYTKgVMJD8VQcE55fOKwzq6ZH6TM&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the Workflows list showing the link icon for copying the URL.")
:   You can make this link accessible for participants to start an agreement
    process themselves. Typically, you use the URL if your workflow is
    configured to start with an anonymous participant in an unmonitored
    scenario.
:   For example, you could post the link on your website for your customers to
    use to start an application process. Or you might use the link in an email
    marketing campaign to encourage prospects to sign up for an event.

Manually in Docusign
:   Use the Run Workflow option from the Workflows
    list more actions menu to start the workflow. When prompted, you can enter a
    unique name for the workflow instance in the Instance
    name field. If you do not add a name, the workflow's default
    instance name is used.
:   Next, complete the workflow steps assigned to the first participant.
    Typically, the first participant is directed to a web form to provide
    participant information for other workflow participants. This method is
    similar to using a template to create an envelope and completing the
    recipient information for any template roles must be defined.
:   You must have Workflows: Prepare permissions to start a
    workflow with this method. ![Screenshot of the Run Workflow option in the action menu for a published workflow.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/runworkflow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU5MTAsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.JQx5-_ZNbAKDXjnBYTKgVMJD8VQcE55fOKwzq6ZH6TM&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the Run Workflow option in the action menu for a published workflow.")

Docusign CLM
:   After you publish the workflow, you create a CLM workflow that
    triggers it.
:   To test this workflow without triggering it from CLM, select Run
    Workflow from the Workflows list more
    actions menu. Enter dummy data for the workflow variables.

From an API Call: Human
:   Select the link icon on the Workflows list page to copy the URL and
    use it to construct the API call. For this start method, the participant
    initiating the workflow will be redirected to the first step of the workflow
    when the API is triggered.
:   To test this workflow without making an API call, select Run
    Workflow from the Workflows list more
    actions menu. Enter dummy data for the workflow variables.

title: How to Start a Workflow
text:
when the API is triggered.
:   To test this workflow without making an API call, select Run
    Workflow from the Workflows list more
    actions menu. Enter dummy data for the workflow variables.

From an API Call: Automated process
:   Select the link icon on the Workflows list page to copy the URL and
    use it to construct the API call. For this start method, an external system
    starts the workflow and not a participant. If there are any participants in
    this workflow, you must invite them with a Set Up
    Invite step.
:   To test this workflow without making an API call, select Run
    Workflow from the Workflows list more
    actions menu. Enter dummy data for the workflow variables.

title: Configure a Collect Data with Web Forms Step
heading_path: Configure a Collect Data with Web Forms Step
text:
Learn how to configure a Collect Data with Web Forms step in an agreement workflow. You use a web forms step to gather information from participants. You can use any existing stand-alone web form on your account that you have access to for your step.

You use a Collect Data with Web Forms step to gather information for your business process. In subsequent workflow steps, you can use any information and documents gathered from your web form to pre-fill document fields, verify identity, and populate other data points.

Consider the following scenarios when you add a web form step to your workflow:

A user within your organization starts the workflow. Start method: Manually in Docusign
:   The preparer knows the identity of a subsequent participant. When they start the workflow, they complete the next participant's information. For this case, add a Collect Data with Web Forms step as the first step of the workflow. Assign it to the preparer participant who will launch the process manually in Docusign. When they start the workflow, they are directed to the web form to provide participant information, and then to any other assigned steps. Data gathered in the web form is available to map to fields in subsequent steps.

An anonymous participant initiates the workflow from a link. Start method: From a Link
:   The participant uses a published link to your workflow. In this self-service scenario (similar to a PowerForm), the workflow starts with a web form step. The step is not mapped to any data. Data gathered in the web form is available to map to fields in subsequent steps.

You can use any existing standalone web form on your account that you have access to. A standalone web form is not tied to a template but exists on its own. Use a standalone web form to gather data and supporting documents from participants for an agreement process. For more information about standalone web forms, see [Web Form Requirements and Data-Mapping Information](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=oha1716325069710.html&_LANG=enus "Review the configuration rules, standalone formatting options, and precise field-matching requirements needed to map data between web forms and agreement templates.").

Before you begin
:   - You must be an account administrator or have Workflows: Create permissions.
    - You must have access to an existing stand-alone web form to use for your workflow step.
    - Your stand-alone web form must be active.

1. Create a new workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus "Learn the basics of how to create an agreement workflow. Process creators and account administrators can create workflows to automate the agreement process.").
2. Select Add a step >  Collect Data with Web Forms.

   The configurator panel for your step opens.
3. Select the form to use and then select Next.

   Note: In the form selection box, at the end of the list of web forms, there is an option Create New Web Form. Use this option if you don't have an existing web form you want to use.

   ![Screenshot of the Web Forms configurator.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/webformsconfig.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU3MjIsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.YBfDeJtkT1vtF_a9V-qyHREWUlKHIXzJSphSlFCmZDc&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the Web Forms configurator.")
4. Specify the participant for this step.

title: Configure a Collect Data with Web Forms Step
heading_path: Configure a Collect Data with Web Forms Step
text:
JleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU3MjIsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.YBfDeJtkT1vtF_a9V-qyHREWUlKHIXzJSphSlFCmZDc&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the Web Forms configurator.")
4. Specify the participant for this step.

   Any pre-existing participants you configured are listed in the dropdown list. To add a new participant, select Add Participant. Depending on your workflow start method, you may also be able to select from these dynamic roles:
   - Workflow Initiator
   - User who last published the workflow
   - User who starts the workflow
5. (Optional) You can map variables to data fields in your web form. This pre-fills web form fields for your participant.
   1. Select Continue to map data fields.
   2. For any web form field, select the variable dropdown. Then select an appropriate variable to map to the web form field.

      Tip: Use the Map for Me option to generate recommended field mappings. Matches are based on field names that are the same or similar. (For example, the name of a starting variable that closely matches a field name on a form.) Review and apply the recommendations or undo the action to try again or use manual mapping.
6. Select Apply.

Your Collect Data with Web Forms step is configured with a web form and a participant. If you mapped data fields, the web form fields will be pre-filled with the relevant data when the participant accesses the web form.

title: Enhance CLM Workflows with Workflow Builder Automation
heading_path: Enhance CLM Workflows with Workflow Builder Automation
text:
Learn how process builders trigger Workflow Builder processes from Docusign CLM to access advanced delivery options and signature features.

To enhance a workflow in Docusign CLM, a process builder can trigger
Workflow Builder processes from a CLM workflow. This integration gives the CLM
workflow access to business activities like WhatsApp delivery, SMS delivery, and more.

Important: For complete information and instructions for
using this functionality, see the CLM documentation about [using Workflow Builder to enhance CLM workflows](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yks1643320936212&topicId=ksq1726176610413.html&_LANG=enus).

This topic describes the basic process:

1. A process builder defines a data model. The model contains information that you want
   to send to CLM when the process completes. The model also specifies where
   you want to send the workflow data in CLM.
2. The process builder creates and publishes a workflow that uses the From Docusign CLM Workflow start method. This workflow can
   contain standard workflow steps.

   Note: The workflow must use the From Docusign CLM Workflow start method.

   To work with CLM, the workflow also
   includes some or all of the following steps:

   Use eSignature API
   :   Lets users select from eSignature APIs to access advanced signature
       features.

   Add Documents to an Envelope
   :   Retrieves documents from CLM and adds them to an envelope.

   Store Files in CLM
   :   Sends signed agreements back to CLM after the envelope completes.

   Send Data to CLM
   :   (Required) Uses your data model to send captured information back to the CLM workflow instance. This step also
       signals that the process is complete.

   Send on someone’s behalf
   :   Requests consent to send eSignature envelopes on behalf of a user other
       than the user who created the Workflow Builder workflow.
3. The process builder defines a payload that a CLM workflow can pass to the
   workflow. The payload contains any required data for invoking eSignature APIs. This
   allows Workflow Builder to execute the desired business activity.
4. The process builder builds and starts a CLM workflow that triggers the
   process and passes the payload. Workflow Builder then executes the business
   activity. For example, you can send agreements through WhatsApp delivery.

title: Configure a Collect Data From an ID Data Step
heading_path: Configure a Collect Data From an ID Data Step
text:
Learn how to configure a Collect ID Data step in an agreement workflow. You use this
step to extract information from a participant's government-issued ID to autofill data in
later workflow steps.

You use a Collect Data From an ID step to gather a participant's identity
data. With this step, you collect information from a participant's government-issued
ID to autofill data in later workflow steps. Participants can use a passport,
government-issued photo ID, or electronic ID (eID) for this step.

Use this step when a participant's information is unknown and you need to gather their identity details. This action automatically extracts information from several fields on their ID, allowing you to autofill fields in later workflow steps. If you want to validate a participant's identity, use the verify
identity step instead. See the topic [Configure a Verify Someone's Identity Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=qht1698126463108.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to configure a Verify Someone's Identity step in an agreement workflow. You use an identity verification step to verify a participant's identity using Docusign's ID Verification process.").

A typical process with this step type might be for your participant to start your
workflow from a link. Next, they present their passport or photo ID. The information gathered during this step populates a web form, which then flows directly into an agreement document for signature.

Depending on the ID type presented, we will attempt to extract the following fields.
Note that some data fields may be missing or incorrect based on the ID type or photo
quality.

- validated
- fullname
- person\_id
- session\_id
- person\_name
- trace\_uid
- provider\_name
- task\_uid
- type\_of\_id
- country\_selected\_by\_signer
- last\_names
- middle\_names
- first\_names
- sex
- date\_of\_birth
- place\_of\_birth
- nationality
- issuing\_country
- issuance\_date
- color\_of\_eyes
- height
- residence\_address
- issuing\_authority
- id\_number
- expiration\_date

You can view details from a completed collect ID data step when monitoring a running
workflow. The Status Details for the step lists key details
for the identity verification activity. This information includes the identification
method, the time the verification was performed, and the result. For information on
how to monitor a workflow process, see the topic [Monitor a Running Workflow Instance](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=vcp1698707326210.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to check the progress of the agreement instances you start from a workflow.").

1. Create a workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus "Learn the basics of how to create an agreement workflow. Process creators and account administrators can create workflows to automate the agreement process.").
2. Select Add a step >  Collect Data From an ID >  Configure.
3. Specify who will participate in this step. Any pre-existing participants you
   configured are listed in the dropdown list. To add a new participant, select
   View or Add Participants.
4. Select Next > Apply.

Your Collect Data From an ID step is configured. You can use
the data gathered in this step in any subsequent steps in your workflow.

title: Move Workflows Between Accounts
text:
Use the export and import functions to copy a workflow between accounts, and even
between Docusign environments.

The ability to export and import workflows is useful when you want to move copies around. You
can use the export and import functions to copy workflows between accounts and between
Docusign environments. You can even use these functions to clone a
workflow within the same account.

Note: Some of the information in this topic refers to new
functionality that may not yet be available for your account.

title: Move Workflows Between Accounts
text:
With the export and import functions, you can create a complete copy of any published
workflow in your account. Here are some common use cases:

From testing to production
:   You create and test a workflow in your Demo account. When you're ready to go
    live with a real business use case, you export the workflow from your Demo
    account and import it to your Production account.

Share with a partner
:   You have a workflow that you want to share with a business partner who has
    their own Docusign account. You export a copy of the
    workflow from your account and provide the downloaded zip file to your
    partner. They upload the workflow to their account.

Create a copy for your own use
:   You have a workflow that you want to preserve and also modify for a new use
    case. You export a copy of the workflow from your account and import it back
    to the same account. This creates a complete draft that you can edit as
    needed without disrupting the original workflow.

title: Move Workflows Between Accounts
text:
When you export a workflow, you create a download with all of the workflow details
and referenced resources. This means that when you later import the workflow into
another account, the step details are preserved. Any resources referenced in the
steps are added to the new account. For example, the web form used in a Collect Data
with Web Forms step.

The imported workflow includes:

- All agreement steps and their configuration details, such as a web form or
  eSignature step.
- All steps for third-party extensions, such as Salesforce read and write
  steps.
- All field mappings configured in workflow steps.
- All web forms and templates used in workflow steps.
- All utility steps, such as branching, confirmation screen, and invitation
  steps.

title: Move Workflows Between Accounts
text:
You must have Workflows: Create or Workflows: Manage permissions to export or
import workflows. If your workflow includes web form or eSignature template steps,
you'll need the permissions to create those assets as well. See the topic [Workflow Builder Roles and Permission Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the different roles involved with creating and using workflows with Docusign Workflow Builder. Each role requires specific user permissions.").

If a workflow includes third-party extension apps, those apps must be installed and
connected to include the step details and configurations when you import it to your
account. Only account administrators can install extension apps.

title: Review and Publish a Workflow
text:
As a process builder, publishing a workflow validates your steps are properly configured and makes the workflow available to your account members. Once published, preparers can start instances of your workflow.

Publishing your workflow validates that the steps are properly configured and makes the workflow available to members on your account to use. As a process builder, you can publish your workflow once you have it fully configured.

Publishing includes these actions:

Check for errors
:   Resolve any reported configuration issues before you can publish the workflow.

Update the workflow name
:   Optional. You can edit the workflow name.

Name instances
:   Optional. By default, instances will be named "[start date]\_[timestamp]\_[instance ID]". You can specify a unique and more easily identifiable name with workflow variables.
:   The default instance name is used in the Workflow Instances list until the instance completes successfully. In progress, canceled, or failed instances retain the default system name.
:   Once an instance is completed, the instance name is updated to use the variable data. See [General Limitations and Considerations for Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=otm1696972807664.html&_LANG=enus "Review important account limits, supported regions, and functional considerations before building automated processes in Workflow Builder.") for the supported data types.
:   Note: If your workflow uses an API start method, the API payload can pass the `instanceName` value. This value will take precedence over the instance name definition configured in the publishing step as described in this topic.

    Note: If your workflow uses the Manually in Docusign start method, the preparer can override the instance name specified here. When the preparer starts the workflow, they get an option to enter an instance name. If they do not enter a value, then the naming you specify here will be used.

Authorize applications
:   Authorize applications to use your account information for the workflow.

Path End steps added
:   Automatic. When you successfully publish a workflow, a Path End step is added to the last step on all paths of your workflow. The Path End utility step has no effect on workflow function, its purpose is merely to help you structure your workflow design.

Important: There is a limit to how many published workflows you can have in your account. See [General Limitations and Considerations for Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=otm1696972807664.html&_LANG=enus "Review important account limits, supported regions, and functional considerations before building automated processes in Workflow Builder.") for details.

1. Log in to your Docusign account and select Agreements >  Workflows.

   The Workflows list shows all workflows that you have access to.
2. Locate the workflow that you want to publish. Filter by Status: Unpublished and Created by: Me.
3. Select the workflow more actions menu and choose Publish  to start the publishing process.

   The workflow opens to the Review before publishing panel.
4. (Optional) Review the workflow name and edit the entry as desired.
5. If the panel shows errors, you must address the configuration errors before you can publish. Address all errors and select Publish to restart the publishing process.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/publishingerrors.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4NzUsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.1KAM_dZGWEQ4BjF7gVm6_ASLuBtqCWOI0cb8aaLbPHE&_LANG=enus)
6. If the panel shows that the workflow is ready to publish, select Next.

   Note:

title: Review and Publish a Workflow
text:
LCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4NzUsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.1KAM_dZGWEQ4BjF7gVm6_ASLuBtqCWOI0cb8aaLbPHE&_LANG=enus)
6. If the panel shows that the workflow is ready to publish, select Next.

   Note:

   If your workflow includes a Verify Someone's Identity step but lacks a Store Files step, a note prompts you to create one. Adding a Store Files step after the Verify Someone's Identity step ensures that ID Evidence sends the identity verification data in a proof file into an archive folder. Read the [Configure a Verify Someone's Identity Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=qht1698126463108.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to configure a Verify Someone's Identity step in an agreement workflow. You use an identity verification step to verify a participant's identity using Docusign's ID Verification process.") page for more information.
7. To make workflow instances more identifiable, add workflow variables to build unique instance names. Then select Done >  Publish.

   ![Screenshot of the publishing step to name workflow instances.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/nameinstances.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4NzUsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.1KAM_dZGWEQ4BjF7gVm6_ASLuBtqCWOI0cb8aaLbPHE&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the publishing step to name workflow instances.")
8. Depending on your workflow steps, you may be required to authorize applications. Select Authorize My Account >  Publish.

   ![Screenshot of the authorization window to publish a workflow.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/authorizeaccountworkflow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4NzUsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.1KAM_dZGWEQ4BjF7gVm6_ASLuBtqCWOI0cb8aaLbPHE&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the authorization window to publish a workflow.")

   Your workflow status updates to "Publishing". Refresh your browser after a few minutes to see the final status "Published". Once published, the workflow is ready for your process preparers to use.

title: Use Pre-Built Templates in Workflow Builder
heading_path: Use Pre-Built Templates in Workflow Builder
text: Discover how pre-built templates in Workflow Builder provide ready-made blueprints to rapidly automate your business and agreement processes.

title: Use Pre-Built Templates in Workflow Builder
heading_path: Use Pre-Built Templates in Workflow Builder
text: Workflow templates are pre-built workflows for specific use cases. You can use these specialized templates to quickly set up, customize, and publish workflows. With workflow templates, you can explore different scenarios for how Workflow Builder can automate your business processes. Use a template as is, or customize it to fit your specific needs.

title: Use Pre-Built Templates in Workflow Builder
heading_path: Use Pre-Built Templates in Workflow Builder
What is included in a workflow template?
text:
Each workflow template comes with pre-built workflow steps that you can customize to adapt to your specific needs.

- The What's required section for each workflow identifies the resources required to use the workflow, including any Docusign products or extension apps. Some workflow templates use third-party extension app steps, such as Salesforce or Google Drive. Links to the Docusign App Center are provided for these.
- The What's included section lists supporting assets like web forms or Docusign eSignature templates that are included with the template. These assets are uploaded automatically to your account when you use the template.

title: Use Pre-Built Templates in Workflow Builder
heading_path: Use Pre-Built Templates in Workflow Builder
Video: How to Use Workflow Templates
text: Here's a short video to introduce you to how you can use workflow templates.

title: Use Pre-Built Templates in Workflow Builder
heading_path: Use Pre-Built Templates in Workflow Builder
Where do I find workflow templates?
text:
Workflow templates are presented in a gallery view in your Docusign IAM account. You can view the available templates through the following ways:

- Go to the Templates  page, and select the Workflow Templates view.
- Go to the Agreements page, and select the  Workflows view. Then select Create Workflow.

![Screenshot of the Workflow Builder Workflow Templates gallery.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/wftemplategallery.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU2NDUsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.r1xYS_Vr9-ReG3PzXliPQG6CHlSGr06AAGaZ3f_TmvM&_LANG=enus)

title: Use Pre-Built Templates in Workflow Builder
heading_path: Use Pre-Built Templates in Workflow Builder
text:
Docusign is developing and adding new templates all the time. The following table lists some of our most widely used templates. Be sure to check the gallery for the latest offerings.

| Template Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Know Your Customer | Collect information such as name and email from any type of participant, and verify their identity with Docusign ID Verification. |
| Mutual NDA with Google Drive | Send Nondisclosure Agreements securely with Docusign, which provides an eSignature that is enforceable and legally binding. Once the agreement has been signed by all parties, a copy is automatically stored in Google Drive. |
| Mutual NDA with OneDrive | Send Nondisclosure Agreements securely with Docusign, which provides an eSignature that is enforceable and legally binding. Once the agreement has been signed by all parties, a copy is automatically stored in OneDrive. |
| Account Change Request - Salesforce | Keep customer accounts up to date in Salesforce by allowing customers to submit and authorize their own address changes. |
| Create Customer in Stripe | Customize a web form for new customers, then use the data to draft and send an invoice through the Stripe platform. |
| W4 Tax Withholding with SharePoint | Have new employees complete and sign a 2024 W-4 Tax Withholding form. Automatically store a copy of the completed form in SharePoint. |
| RFI/NDA with Salesforce | Customize a web form for potential customers to request information about your business. Create and track each lead within Salesforce CRM. |
| Glyue Change of Address | Verify an account holder's identity to then search and update the banking core with the provided address information. |
| HR Offer Letter | Generate an offer letter and save a signed copy in OneDrive. |
| Wire Transfer | Route a wire transfer request after verifying a customer's identity. |
| Bank Account Opening | Gather information from a new customer to open a bank account. Verify the applicant's identity before sending an account application to sign. |
| Right to Work (UK) | Checks and validates passport information to check a person's legal eligibility to work in the United Kingdom and Northern Ireland. |

title: Configuration Details for Complex Branching Rules
heading_path: Configuration Details for Complex Branching Rules
text:
Read about nesting IF statements and using advanced operators in a branching rule workflow step.

This topic provides information on creating more complex branching rules and provides some illustrative examples.

title: Configuration Details for Complex Branching Rules
heading_path: Configuration Details for Complex Branching Rules
text:
You can design a workflow with multiple nested branching rules. You construct nested IFs by adding branching rules to the outcomes of another branching rule. Nested IFs support more complex conditional logic than just a single rule that provides a binary, TRUE/FALSE outcome. You can nest up to eight levels of rules.

If you delete a branching rule, you select whether to keep the True or False path steps in your workflow. You can also choose to delete all steps from both paths.

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/delete_rule_steps_choice.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MzQsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.5lqtNMJHcOV58i04qkKTeAqk0locHsUib65JNU9r0AU&_LANG=enus)

title: Configuration Details for Complex Branching Rules
heading_path: Configuration Details for Complex Branching Rules
text:
You can configure a single branching rule with up to five AND or OR conditions. You can mix AND and OR conditions to build multiple groups of conditions.

- A rule with multiple AND conditions evaluates to true if the conditions are all true, and false if any conditions are false.
- A rule with multiple OR conditions evaluates to true if any of the conditions are true, and false if all conditions are false.
- A rule with a mix of AND and OR conditions evaluates to true if any group of conditions evaluates to true, and false if all groups are false.

![Screenshot of a branching rule configuration with two groups of conditions.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/Branching-rule-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MzQsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.5lqtNMJHcOV58i04qkKTeAqk0locHsUib65JNU9r0AU&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of a branching rule configuration with two groups of conditions.")

title: Configuration Details for Complex Branching Rules
heading_path: Configuration Details for Complex Branching Rules
text:
Branching rules support advanced conditionality logic. The available conditions depend on the data type you are using for your rule.

Booleans
:   For variables that are boolean and have only two possible values, the branching rule operators are `Is` and `Is not`. The results are `True` or `False`.
:   Examples:

    - Checkbox field is checked or unchecked.
    - Radio button is selected or unselected.
    - Identity verification check passed or failed.

Strings
:   For supported field types (text, note, email, name) that can contain string values, the standard comparison operators are provided. These can evaluate if the variable value equals, contains, starts with, or ends with a specified result.
:   Examples:

    - Text field contains the value `California`.
    - Email field ends with `.org`.
    - Text field does not equal the value `New Jersey`.![Screenshot of the rule operators for a string comparison.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/advancedruleoperators.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MzQsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.5lqtNMJHcOV58i04qkKTeAqk0locHsUib65JNU9r0AU&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the rule operators for a string comparison.")

Dropdowns
:   For dropdown field types from a web form or eSignature step, standard comparison operators are provided to evaluate if the variable value equals a specified dropdown value. To select the result the rule will look for, the rule configuration presents the list of dropdown values from the mapped field to select from. ![Screenshot of configuration for a rule checking the value of a dropdown field from a web form step.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/dropdown_rule.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MzQsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.5lqtNMJHcOV58i04qkKTeAqk0locHsUib65JNU9r0AU&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of configuration for a rule checking the value of a dropdown field from a web form step.")

Dates
:   For date fields originating from a web form, eSignature step, instance date, or a starting variable, comparisons of equality, inequality, and dynamic comparison are supported. For web form date fields, use the (Normalized) version of the field. For other variables, you can select any variable of type = date for a comparison.![Screenshot of a rule evaluating a date field and the list of available operators.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/conditional_rule_date_operators.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MzQsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.5lqtNMJHcOV58i04qkKTeAqk0locHsUib65JNU9r0AU&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of a rule evaluating a date field and the list of available operators.")

Numbers
:   For number fields originating from a web form or a starting variable, the standard numerical comparisons are supported. For web form number fields, use the (Normalized) version of the field. For starting variables, you can select any variable of type = numbers for a numerical comparison. ![Screenshot of the configuration of a branching rule for a numerical comparison.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/branchingrulenumericalcomparison.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MzQsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.5lqtNMJHcOV58i04qkKTeAqk0locHsUib65JNU9r0AU&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the configuration of a branching rule for a numerical comparison.")

title: Configuration Details for Complex Branching Rules
heading_path: Configuration Details for Complex Branching Rules
text:
4iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MzQsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.5lqtNMJHcOV58i04qkKTeAqk0locHsUib65JNU9r0AU&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the configuration of a branching rule for a numerical comparison.")

Currency
:   Currency fields can originate from starting variables or from data gathered through steps in your workflow, such as a web form or eSignature envelope. When mapping a currency field, there are three versions of the field. You can use any one of them in a branching rule.

    - **Currency field name**: contains the currency code and numeric amount
    - **Currency field name (Double Value)**: contains the numeric amount
    - **Currency field name (Currency Code)**: contains the currency code

      Note: If your branching rule evaluates the full currency field rather than its individual components (`Double Value` or `Currency Code`), the rule requires all parts of the currency data to match your criteria. All conditions must evaluate to true for the overall outcome to be true.

      ![Screenshot of a branching rule configured to check if TotalContractValue is greater than or equal to $1,000.00 USD.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/currency_branching_rule_ex.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MzQsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.5lqtNMJHcOV58i04qkKTeAqk0locHsUib65JNU9r0AU&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of a branching rule configured to check if TotalContractValue is greater than or equal to $1,000.00 USD.")

      This example illustrates a branching rule that checks the full currency field for the value and the code.

title: Configuration Details for Complex Branching Rules
heading_path: Configuration Details for Complex Branching Rules
text:
You can configure a branching rule to merge the True and False branches. This is optional and useful if you want to combine the paths to use a single step outcome. You can add steps to each path to customize the workflow based on the initial outcome of the rule.

Note: You can also merge a multi branch rule. A multi branch rule can have up to 15 branches. You can merge all branches, or any two or more branches.

Important things to consider when opting to merge a branching rule:

- Variables and files generated from steps inside of a merged branching rule will not be available to use in steps occurring after the merge. Do not merge a rule if you are gathering data or generating files you want to use later in the workflow.
- You can nest another branching rule within the merged paths. The paths of a nested branching rule will also merge into a single outcome.
- You can edit the step to remove the merge setting. Any steps in the rule occurring before the merge point are retained on the branch where they exist. For any steps after the merge, you can delete them or select which path to move them to.

  Figure 1. Workflow example with a merged branching rule

  ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/workflow_example_merged_branching.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MzQsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.5lqtNMJHcOV58i04qkKTeAqk0locHsUib65JNU9r0AU&_LANG=enus)

title: Unpublish or Delete a Workflow
text:
As a
process
builder, you can unpublish any of your paused workflows. You can edit and
republish unpublished workflows, or you can permanently delete them.

As a
process
builder, you can unpublish any of your paused workflows. Pausing a
workflow allows any running instances to complete. Once all instances are completed
or canceled, you can unpublish the workflow.

Unpublishing
a workflow helps you manage the quota of active workflows you can have on your
account. If you need to free up capacity, you can pause and unpublish any workflows
you don't need.
Unpublished
workflows do not count towards your account's quota.

Important: If you republish a previously unpublished
workflow, a new workflow URL will be generated for it. You will need to update any
distributed or embedded URLs to the new workflow URL.

You can also permanently delete your unpublished workflows. You cannot recover a deleted
workflow.

1. Log in to your Docusign account and select Agreements >  Workflows.

   The Workflows list shows all workflows that you
   have access to.
2. Locate the workflow you want to unpublish. Filter by Status:
   Published and Created by: Me.
3. Select the workflow more actions menu and choose
   Pause New Instances.

   Your workflow status changes to "Paused". Any running instances will
   continue but no new instances can be started. Once all instances are complete or
   canceled, you can unpublish the workflow.
4. To unpublish the paused workflow, first confirm that there are no running
   instances.
   1. Select View Instances for the workflow.
   2. For any in progress instance, select
      Cancel.![Screenshot of a running instance with the action menu expanded showing the Cancel option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/cancelrunninginstance.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4ODUsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.U7e0NccLk0h9LyzGrDBvUT5g5ZFh8dW0nXAAaxn9nG4&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of a running instance with the action menu expanded showing the Cancel option.")
   3. Return to the Workflows list.
5. Select the workflow's more actions menu and choose
   Unpublish.
6. In the confirmation window, select Unpublish.

   Your workflow status changes to "Unpublished".
7. (Optional)
   To delete an unpublished workflow, select the workflow more actions menu >  Delete and confirm your action in the confirmation window.

Your workflow is unpublished. If you chose to delete it, it is removed from your account and cannot be recovered.

title: Configure a Send an Email Step
heading_path: Configure a Send an Email Step
text:
Learn how to add an email notification step to your agreement workflow. This step
generates an ad hoc email notification to convey information to any workflow participant or
other named individual.

As part of a workflow, you can automatically generate an ad hoc email notification.
The email notification step is useful to convey information to one or more
individuals at a particular point in the workflow.

For the notification, you can hardcode recipients, use starting variables, or any other email
addresses gathered in prior workflow steps. You can include up to 10 recipients in
an email notification step.

These automated emails are customized with your account's default signing brand to maintain a professional and trusted appearance. If no default brand is defined, standard Docusign branding is used.

This step generates an email message to the specified recipients. The email comes from the
sender "Docusign Workflows" at "workflows@docusign.net". All step
recipients receive the same email message. If you want to generate unique messages,
add a separate step for each message you want to send.

Some example use cases for this step:

- Notify a customer that their account application was approved.
- Notify an internal stakeholder about the status of a workflow instance.

1. Create a new workflow as described in the topic [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus "Learn the basics of how to create an agreement workflow. Process creators and account administrators can create workflows to automate the agreement process.").
2. At the point in the workflow where you want to send the email, select Add a step >  Send an Email >  Configure.

   The configuration panel for your step opens for you to add the recipients
   and email message.
3. Add up to 10 email recipients. You can select any workflow email variable, or
   you can add hardcoded email addresses.
4. Compose the message. Add a subject, headline, and body text.

   For the body text, use the rich text formatting and add workflow variables to create engaging, personalized content for your recipients. Type "/" to select a workflow variable.

   ![Screenshot of the configuration panel for the Send an Email step.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/sendemailconfigurator.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MjcsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.1lDm-QyWGxZVs-hrFUvw2MRk3ZyxZ8aELEj1ve1Ilxs&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the configuration panel for the Send an Email step.")
5. (Optional) Select the Preview tab to see how your message will appear.
6. Select Apply.

Your Configure a Send Email Notification step is configured.

title: Configure a Set Up Invite Step
heading_path: Configure a Set Up Invite Step
text:
Learn how to use a Set Up Invite step in your agreement workflow
to invite participants to join the workflow.

You use the Set Up Invite
step type to invite a participant to your workflow. This step can generate invitations
to the participant through email, SMS text message, or WhatsApp. The invitation includes a link to enter the workflow, allowing the participant to engage in a subsequent workflow step. The link is valid for one year and cannot be resent.

Note: Email invitations are issued from the sender "Docusign Workflows" at "workflows@docusign.net". Docusign applies your account's default signing brand to all notification steps—including Set up Invite, Send an Email, and Send Documents for Signature—as well as the signing session itself. If no default brand is defined, standard Docusign branding is used.

For all
invited participants, you must provide values as required for name, email address, and phone number. You can map workflow variables for the required participant information, or hardcode values. Once
defined, these variables are set at the workflow level and will be referenced when
mapping the same participant in any subsequent step.

Note: Account settings must allow for SMS or WhatsApp delivery to use these delivery methods. Your
account administrator manages these settings in Admin > Sending Settings > Fields and Properties To use both SMS text message and email delivery methods
together, your account administrator must also ensure the Allow SMS
delivery to recipients user permission is enabled for your user
profile. If you are unable to follow the steps in this topic, contact your
account administrator to enable these options. Read more about these methods in
the topic [Multi-Channel
Delivery for Notifications: SMS and WhatsApp](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gav1643676262430&topicId=kwk1607393003979.html&_LANG=enus).

Table 1. Workflow start methods and Set Up Invite step requirements

| Start Method | First Participant | Additional Participants |
| --- | --- | --- |
| From a Link | Enters workflow automatically | Needs an invitation step to enter the workflow |
| Manually in Docusign | Enters workflow automatically | Needs an invitation step to enter the workflow |
| From an API Call - Human | Enters workflow automatically | Needs an invitation step to enter the workflow |
| From an API Call - Automated process | Needs an invitation step | Needs an invitation step to enter the workflow |
| From Docusign CLM Workflow | Needs an invitation step | Needs an invitation step to enter the workflow |

1. Create a workflow.
2. Select Add a step >  Set Up Invite >  Configure. Select a participant and choose the desired delivery channel.
   This is the participant who will receive the invitation to your workflow.
3. (Optional) For email delivery, you can select the option Include
   custom message and input your own email message to the
   participant.
4. Select
   Apply.
5. Complete any missing participant information. Select the variables to use for
   the name, email address, and phone number from any prior workflow step, or enter fixed values manually.

   ![Screenshot of the configuration window for a Set Up Invite step.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/set_up_invite_config.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MjIsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.usR7EH4pph44QfBpnyF3nRhh_0SeIUfirKhPTudHUzE&_LANG=enus)

   The participant's information typically comes from any prior steps, such as
   a Collect Data with Web Forms or Collect Data
   From an ID step. For CLM and API workflow starts, these may be
   incoming variables from an external system.
6. Select Apply.

title: Configure a Set Up Invite Step
heading_path: Configure a Set Up Invite Step
text:
prior steps, such as
   a Collect Data with Web Forms or Collect Data
   From an ID step. For CLM and API workflow starts, these may be
   incoming variables from an external system.
6. Select Apply.

Your Set Up Invite step is configured. When the workflow reaches this step, an invitation is sent to the participant to join the workflow. The
invitation uses the selected delivery channel (for example, email or SMS text message).
The link in the invitation is valid for one year and cannot be resent. For email delivery, if you specified a custom message, it is included in the
communication.

title: ID Evidence in Workflow Builder
text:
Read about ID Evidence integration with Workflow Builder and how it helps deliver evidence of the ID verification process.

ID Evidence stores data captured during the Verify Someone’s Identity or Risk Assessment steps. For example, during a Verify Someone’s Identity step, ID Evidence can collect data such as the participant’s name or address.

Process builders use ID Evidence to track data in the step process. They can also use the collected data to comply with KYC (Know Your Customer), AML (Anti-Money Laundering) requirements, as well as internal regulations.

title: ID Evidence in Workflow Builder
text:
When a participant goes through a Verify Someone’s Identity or a Risk Assessment step, ID Evidence collects the data that the administrator configured. For example, in a Verify Someone’s Identity step, ID Evidence can collect the participant’s name, the identity verification result, and the identity document submitted.

Once the participant completes the step, the workflow proceeds with the following steps until it reaches the Store files step. At this stage, the system exports the ID Evidence data as a PDF to a storage provider, according to the Store files step configuration. For example, if the process builder added a Store files in Google Drive step, ID Evidence exports the PDF file to the configured directory.

title: ID Evidence in Workflow Builder
heading_path: ID Evidence configuration with Workflow Builder
text: To store identity data from a workflow using ID Evidence, process builders must use a Verify Someone’s Identity or a Risk Assessment step. Process builders must configure those steps to authorize data storage. In addition, they must add a Store files step after the Verify Someone’s Identity or the Risk Assessment step. Properly configuring the Store files step ensures that the ID Evidence data goes to the correct directory. For more information about configuring data storage for Verify Someone’s Identity steps, read the [Identity Verification Configurations](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=eya1583277454804.html&_LANG=enus) help pages. Read the [Configure a Risk Assessment Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=tsk6ecf58be-0285-4608-92cd-66252f6e07a5.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to configure the Risk Assessment step in a workflow. This step gathers participant risk signals for dynamic orchestration, data-driven decisions, and simplified compliance.") page for more information about Risk Assessment steps data storage.

title: ID Evidence in Workflow Builder
text:
Process builders can use the following storage providers when configuring a Store files step to export ID Evidence data:

- Google Drive
- Box
- Dropbox
- OneDrive
- SharePoint

title: ID Evidence in Workflow Builder
text:
The data that ID Evidence collects depends on the data storage configuration selected for your step.

There are two types of data ID Evidence can collect in a Verify Someone’s Identity step: default data and configurable data. Default data includes the status of identity verification and the name of the provider that verified the ID document. It does not provide personally identifiable information (PII). Configurable data is additional information that you can store in ID Evidence. For example, if the process builder uses the Liveness Detection for ID Verification configuration, they can add the selfie video to the ID Evidence stored data.

The process builder is responsible for adding configurable data to the ID Evidence collection. They decide which additional information, including personally identifiable information (PII), to store. Docusign cannot be held responsible if PII is accessible within the configurable data.

From a GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) perspective, the process builder is the "data controller" and determines what data to collect and store. Docusign, the "data processor", operates on behalf of its customers to store and manage the data according to the administrator's configuration.

title: ID Evidence in Workflow Builder
text:
ID Evidence has the following limitations when collecting data from a workflow step:

- The redaction of ID Evidence data is not available
- The Evidence data is not available through the API
- The Phone Authentication configuration does not generate ID Evidence data in the Verify Someone’s Identity step

title: ID Evidence in Workflow Builder
text: By default, ID Evidence keeps its data forever. You can access your data as long as you have access to your Docusign account. The location of your ID Evidence data depends on your Docusign account. For example, European accounts have their data stored in Europe.

title: Configure a Branching Rule
text:
You can create conditional rules in your agreement workflow. These rules validate a
condition and branch the workflow based on whether the condition is met or not.

A branching rule creates a true/false step. You can add a branching rule after any step (such as
identity verification). After the branching rule, you define the next action for
whether the rule is met (true) or not met (false).

As an example, you might add a branching rule after a Verify Someone's Identity step. If the participant
passes the identity verification, they continue to the next step in the agreement
process, such as a Send Documents for Signature step. If they fail the identity verification, you
end the workflow with a confirmation step explaining that they must retry or contact
you for assistance.

![Screenshot of a workflow example with a branching rule that evaluates an identity verification step result.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/branchingruleexample.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MzEsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.9gslAu5SY6FTguvcODeHj9n0zT-9VhWD5m37EpyqGJQ&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of a workflow example with a branching rule that evaluates an identity verification step result.")

Branching rules support a variety of advanced conditionality options:

- Nest up to eight levels of branching rules. Use nested rules to test for multiple criteria,
  making your workflow more flexible and powerful.
- Add AND or OR conditions. Configure a single branching rule with up to five AND or OR
  conditions.
- Use advanced operators to create more precise conditional logic statements.

You can add branching rules to your workflow as you build it. You can also insert an
Add a Branching Rule step between two steps. If you do
this, you specify to which branch to move all steps that follow.

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/gifinsertbranchingrule.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MzEsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.9gslAu5SY6FTguvcODeHj9n0zT-9VhWD5m37EpyqGJQ&_LANG=enus)

Similarly, if you delete a branching rule, you select whether to keep to True or
False path steps in your workflow. You can also choose to delete all steps from both
paths.

For more information about complex logic for branching rules, see [Configuration Details for Complex Branching Rules](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=jaf1716918359288.html&_LANG=enus "Read about nesting IF statements and using advanced operators in a branching rule workflow step.").

1. Create a workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus "Learn the basics of how to create an agreement workflow. Process creators and account administrators can create workflows to automate the agreement process.").
2. Select Add a step >  Add a Branching Rule.

   - If you are adding the step between existing steps, select which path to
     move the steps that come after the new rule. Then select
     Configure.
   - If you are adding the step to the end of your workflow, select
     Configure.

   The configuration panel for the branching rule opens.
3. Select the variable for the rule to check, the operator for the rule, and the
   result for the rule. Then select Done.

title: Configure a Branching Rule
text:
ect
     Configure.

   The configuration panel for the branching rule opens.
3. Select the variable for the rule to check, the operator for the rule, and the
   result for the rule. Then select Done.

   Example: Add a branching rule after a Verify Someone's Identity step to
   determine the next step based on whether the participant successfully validates
   their identity.
   - For what to check, select the identity step and the variable
     `Validated`.
   - For the operator, select `Is`.
   - For the result, select `True`.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/branch_rule_example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MzEsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.9gslAu5SY6FTguvcODeHj9n0zT-9VhWD5m37EpyqGJQ&_LANG=enus)Example: Add a branching rule after a web forms step to determine the next
   step based on the data entered in the form.
   - For what to check, select the web forms step and the form variable to
     evaluate.
   - For the operator, select one of the comparisons, such as `Equal
     to`, `Contains`, or `Does not start
     with`.
   - For the result, enter the value to compare to.
4. (Optional) Select + AND or + OR
   to add conditions to your rule. You can configure up to five conditions.
5. (Optional) Select the option Merge true and false branches? if you want to combine the branches to continue the workflow. This optional setting is useful if you want to combine the paths to use a single step outcome.

   Important: Variables and files generated from steps inside of a merged branching rule will not be available to use in steps occurring after the merge. For more details, see [Merging branches](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=jaf1716918359288.html&anchor=jaf1716918359288__section-426&_LANG=enus).
6. Select Apply.

Your branching rule is configured. Add the next steps for your
branching rule, depending on whether the rule was met (True) or
not (False).

title: Data Types for Workflow Variables
text:
Look up details about the data types that you can use to pass data to workflow variables.

When initiating a process from an external application or API payload, you must define the correct data type for each starting variable. Mapping precise data types ensures that incoming strings, numbers, or JSON objects parse correctly across subsequent workflow steps.

There are different types of data that you can define for a starting variable. Each of these supports specific input values.

title: Data Types for Workflow Variables
text:
All variables have the following character limits:

- Variable name: 150 characters
- Hard-coded variable (fixed value entered in step configuration):
  - name: 150 characters
  - value: 4000 characters
- Input limit at workflow start: 4000 characters

title: Data Types for Workflow Variables
heading_path: Supported data types and input specifications
text:
Text
:   Supports alphanumeric and special characters. Text variables are suitable for a variety of strings, such as names, addresses, and other details with separators, such as dashes or currency symbols.

Email
:   Supports valid email formats. Example: marcus.chen@mail.com

Phone Number
:   Requires a country code and phone number when manually entering a fixed value during workflow step configuration. Extension is optional. If sending values by API from an external system, adhere to the following JSON structure:

    ```
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    |  | { |
    |  | countryCode:'1', |
    |  | number: '1234567890', |
    |  | extension: '1234', |
    |  | } |
    ```

Date
:   When manually entering a fixed date during workflow step configuration, the required formats are locale-specific (defined by your account's regional Admin settings). If triggering the process from an external API payload, inputs must adhere to ISO 8601 standard patterns:

    - YYYY-MM-DD (defaults to midnight UTC)
    - YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ
    - YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss±HH:mm
    - YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.SZ
    - YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.SSZ
    - YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.SSSZ
    - YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.S±HH:mm
    - YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.SS±HH:mm
    - YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.SSS±HH:m

Currency
:   Supports a numeric amount and a corresponding ISO currency code. Use this for storing and reusing monetary values (for example, `1000.00 USD`).

    If sending values by API from an external system, adhere to the following format:

    ```
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    |  | { |
    |  | currencyCode: "USD", |
    |  | doubleValue: 1234.56 |
    |  | } |
    ```

Numbers
:   Supports numbers up to 16 digits. Values can include localized group and decimal separators in addition to the digits. Examples: 1,234.5 in US English. 1 234,5 in French.

Object
:   Supports any valid JSON object (key-value pairs surrounded by curly braces and separated by commas). Use this data type to pull in complex, grouped data packets from external systems to distribute to downstream workflow steps.

    Note: This data type is commonly used to handle structured data outputs and custom header mappings when configuring a [Make a Web Request](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=task-8583.html&_LANG=enus "Use a Make a Web Request step to call an external or internal service from Workflow Builder to look up data or push an update without a dedicated app.") step.

Array
:   Supports any valid JSON array (ordered elements enclosed in square brackets). Use this data type when a workflow step needs to process a list of repeating items sequentially.

    Note: When using a [Make a Web Request](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=task-8583.html&_LANG=enus "Use a Make a Web Request step to call an external or internal service from Workflow Builder to look up data or push an update without a dedicated app.") step, you can use arrays to pass lists of repeating items to an external API or to parse multi-item lists returned by an external service.

title: Web Form Requirements and Data-Mapping Information
heading_path: Web Form Requirements and Data-Mapping Information
text:
Review the configuration rules, standalone formatting options, and precise field-matching requirements needed to map data between web forms and agreement templates.

Using a web form in a workflow carries certain requirements for the type of web form used. There are also details that the process builder should keep in mind when mapping data between a web form and a template.

title: Web Form Requirements and Data-Mapping Information
heading_path: Web Form Requirements and Data-Mapping Information
text:
You can use any existing stand-alone web form on your account that you have access to. A stand-alone web form is not tied to a template but exists on its own to use to gather data for an agreement process.

There are two ways to create stand-alone web forms.

Create a blank form
:   Create a new web form that is not associated with a template. On the Templates page, create a new web form. Use the Create Blank Form instead of selecting a template to use.
:   Set up your web form pages and add the questions to show to participants. Consider customizing the API reference name for each question. Creating a meaningful name can help process creators correctly identify and use the web form data fields in workflow steps.

Create a workflow version from an existing web form
:   Copy an existing web form to create a stand-alone version. On the Templates page, locate the web form you want to copy. Select the more actions menu and select Create Workflow Version. ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/createworkflowversion.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU3MjQsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.w92jOosELBJjNtP9eX1GNeOo0JnFsVZYmGZJQ8DFjdw&_LANG=enus)
:   The new web form is added to your list of web forms, named "Workflow Copy - [original web form name]". The web form is stand-alone and is not associated with any template.
:   Activate the web form to make it available to use in a workflow. Select Open >  Activate.

title: Web Form Requirements and Data-Mapping Information
heading_path: Web Form Requirements and Data-Mapping Information
Mapping data between web forms and templates
text:
In a workflow, you can use web forms to gather data from participants. As an option, you can map data from other sources to prepopulate web form fields. This data can be strings for values like text, dates, email addresses, and phone numbers, and also numerical values.

**Currency**

When a web form uses a currency field type, the submitted value becomes a currency variable in Workflow Builder. You can map currency variables to Number (Currency) fields in templates and you can use them in branching rules.

Web form currency fields can also receive currency data from other sources, such as starting variables in a workflow that uses an API start method.

When mapping a currency field, there are three versions of the field:

- **Currency field name**: contains the currency code and numeric amount
- **Currency field name (Double Value)**: contains the numeric amount
- **Currency field name (Currency Code)**: contains the currency code

**Checkboxes, radio buttons, and dropdowns**

You can also use checkboxes, radio buttons, and dropdowns in your web form. For these field types, to map data between a web form and an eSignature template, there are some specific requirements. To ensure values for these field types populate correctly, the following value names must match exactly.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Field type** | **eSignature template** | **Web form** |
| Checkbox | Checkbox Values | API value for each checkbox |
| Radio Button | Radio Button Values | API value |
| Dropdown | Values | API value |

title: Web Form Requirements and Data-Mapping Information
heading_path: Web Form Requirements and Data-Mapping Information
text:
Workflow Builder supports stand-alone web forms with attachment fields. Participants can use attachment fields to upload files through the web form. You can map these attachment fields in a Send Documents for Signature as **supporting documents**. Supporting documents are ancillary files with no recipient roles or fields.

Some important points to note:

- Supporting documents cannot be used as the primary document for signature.
- Web form attachment fields can be mapped only to a Send Documents for Signature step; you cannot map it to an attachment field on an eSignature template.

Read about how to create a web form with an attachment field in [Build a Web Form with Attachment Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=ymm1721679198157.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how to build or update a Web Form with attachment fields or import them from a template that includes attachments.").

title: General Limitations and Considerations for Workflow Builder
heading_path: General Limitations and Considerations for Workflow Builder
text:
Review important account limits, supported regions, and functional considerations before building automated processes in Workflow Builder.

Workflow Builder is a new platform to help you build and deploy customized workflows that automate and accelerate your agreement processes without writing any code. As you explore this exciting new offering, there are some general limitations and other considerations you should be aware of. Docusign will continue to develop the platform, and potentially address some of these limitations in future releases.

In addition to these general limitations, if you use a workflow step to get signatures you should review the topic [Template Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=xnw1709852160602.html&_LANG=enus "Important information for process builders to consider before starting to develop workflows that include a Prepare eSignature Template step. When you prepare a template to use in a Send Documents for Signature step, there are several requirements and limitations that must be respected to configure the step successfully.").

title: General Limitations and Considerations for Workflow Builder
heading_path: General Limitations and Considerations for Workflow Builder
text:
Supported regions and languages
:   Workflow Builder is available globally in all supported sending languages.

FedRAMP and GovRAMP certification compliance
:   For customers in regulated industries, Workflow Builder and its pre-built templates are FedRAMP and GovRAMP certified. However, for these account types, the following Workflow Builder features are not supported:

    - Verify Someone's Identity step
    - Collect Data from an ID step
    - SMS and WhatsApp delivery methods for notifications

Number of workflow definitions
:   Your organization's account plan determines how many published and paused workflows you can have at a time. Once your account reaches this limit, you must unpublish a workflow before you can publish a new one. There is no limit on the number of draft workflows that you can have.

Standalone web forms
:   Workflow data can be gathered using web forms. Use a standalone web form for this action. Web forms created from templates are not supported. See the information on standalone web forms in the topic [Web Form Requirements and Data-Mapping Information](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=oha1716325069710.html&_LANG=enus "Review the configuration rules, standalone formatting options, and precise field-matching requirements needed to map data between web forms and agreement templates.") for more information.

Data types and character limits for variables
:   For workflows with start methods designed to initiate the workflow from an external system, you can specify variables. These variables receive payload data from the external system. For example, your customer starts a workflow from your website to submit an application. They've logged in to their account, so when they start the application, the system passes their name, contact information, and account number into the process.

    There are several different types of data that you can define for a starting variable. Each of these supports specific input values. For details on the supported data types, see the help topic [Data Types for Workflow Variables](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=ogx1738026109020.html&_LANG=enus "Look up details about the data types that you can use to pass data to workflow variables.").

Instance names
:   You can specify unique workflow instance names by using workflow variables. Instance names can be defined in the publishing configuration for a workflow. For workflows that use an API start method, the API payload can pass the `instanceName` value. The `instanceName` value takes precedence over the instance name defined in the workflow publishing configuration.
:   The following variable types are supported for constructing an instance name:
:   - Strings
    - Numbers
    - Dates
    - Email
    - Phone
    - Boolean

Direct signing account prerequisites and 21 CFR Part 11 accounts
:   Direct signing is an option for the first signer in a Send Documents for Signature step to go directly from a prior workflow step (such as a web form or ID Verification step) into the signing session. To configure a Send Documents for Signature step for direct signing, your Docusign account must have the **Enable In Session Signing** setting turned on. If this feature is not turned on for your account, workflows using direct signing will fail at that step with the following error:

    ```
    "This Account lacks sufficient permissions. In-Session permission required when specifying a captive recipient."
    ```

    If you need access to this capability, contact Docusign Support or your account team for assistance.

    Direct signing is not available for Docusign IAM accounts with a Life Sciences Part 11 module.

title: General Limitations and Considerations for Workflow Builder
heading_path: General Limitations and Considerations for Workflow Builder
text:
If you need access to this capability, contact Docusign Support or your account team for assistance.

    Direct signing is not available for Docusign IAM accounts with a Life Sciences Part 11 module.

Workflow sizing and resource limits
:   Workflows do not have a hard step maximum, but rather an internal size limit based on total underlying system actions. Standard workflows comfortably support 30 to 40 steps, but resource-intensive actions—such as Send Documents for Signature or Collect Data with Web Forms—consume more platform capacity and may lower your total allowed step count. If a workflow design exceeds these internal resource thresholds, a size warning or error will appear on the canvas at publish time to prevent deployment.

title: Export a Workflow
text: Learn about exporting a published workflow from your account.

title: Export a Workflow
text:
- For some workflows, you may encounter this error when you attempt to export:
  ![Screenshot of an error message when exporting an older published workflow.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/exportworkflowrepublisherror.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4OTIsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.JVZ93dEjc0HZoEILMwz2vxEp8wnnccHVcRKcXRG6SCE&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of an error message when exporting an older published workflow.")

  If this happens, you must republish the workflow. This may require you to
  edit the workflow to conform to current requirements.
- You must have Workflows: Create permissions to export
  workflows.

You can export any published workflow available in your account.

1. Log in to your Docusign account and go to Agreements >  Workflows.
2. Locate the published workflow that you want to export. Use the
   Status filter to get a list of your published
   workflows.
3. In the more actions menu for the workflow, select
   Export.
