   ![Screenshot of the menu control to export a workflow.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/exportworkflowmenu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4OTIsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.JVZ93dEjc0HZoEILMwz2vxEp8wnnccHVcRKcXRG6SCE&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the menu control to export a workflow.")
4. In the Export this workflow? confirmation screen, select
   Export.

A ZIP file is generated and exported to your local downloads
folder. The ZIP file name is the workflow name. The file contains the workflow and all
of the configuration details.

title: Export a Workflow
text:
Use the exported ZIP file to [import the workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=zoi1742412999176.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to import a workflow into your account.") to another account. You can
also import it to the same account to create a complete copy of the workflow.

title: Build Workflows to Automate the Agreement Process
heading_path: Build Workflows to Automate the Agreement Process
text:
Read about the process of building agreement workflows with Docusign Workflow Builder.

As a process builder, you design and build workflows to automate your agreement processes.
Workflow Builder empowers you to design and run workflows that combine steps from multiple Docusign apps and third-party extensions. Bring disconnected agreement
tools together in Workflow Builder's no-code workflow designer.

title: Build Workflows to Automate the Agreement Process
heading_path: Build Workflows to Automate the Agreement Process
text:
Workflow Builder is ideal for high-volume, business-to-consumer (B2C) agreements. In these use cases,
the customer wants to implement processes quickly without engaging costly IT
services. Use cases of this nature are supported by the currently available Docusign applications and partner app extensions.

Here are some basic use case examples of how you could use Workflow Builder to automate your agreement
processes:

Opening a customer account
:   A credit union wants to design a digital and automated process to open a new account for a
    customer. The process will first verify the customer's identity, then
    present a form to gather information for the new account. Finally a
    pre-filled account application is presented to the customer for signature.

Adding a beneficiary to a customer's account
:   A wealth management firm needs a digitized process to help a customer add a
    beneficiary to their account. The customer completes a form to provide
    beneficiary data. From there, a completed beneficiary application is sent to
    the customer to sign. The completed application is archived in the firm's
    Google Drive file system.

New hire onboarding
:   A large enterprise firm wants an automated process to verify each new hire's
    identity and then send an employment offer letter.

Automated Contract Renewal
:   An organization wants to achieve zero-touch contract renewals. A CRM system checks for expiring agreements and, 30 days before expiration, triggers a workflow through an API call. The workflow seamlessly creates an Agreement Desk renewal request automatically and unattended, pulling in all necessary variables without any human intervention.

External Vendor Intake
:   A procurement team needs a streamlined way to accept incoming requests from outside vendors. An external, non-Docusign user clicks a public web link to initiate an interactive intake form. Once submitted, the workflow automatically generates a complete Agreement Desk request for the internal team to review.

title: Build Workflows to Automate the Agreement Process
heading_path: Build Workflows to Automate the Agreement Process
text:
To illustrate what Workflow Builder can do for you, let's review a basic scenario for an automated and
fully digital agreement process. In this scenario we will use several of the available
Docusign applications and an external file archive app. The
agreement process will have the following steps:

Step 1: Gather information from your customer
:   Using Web Forms, your customer provides their personal information needed to complete your
    agreement.

Step 2: Verify your customer's identity
:   Docusign ID Verification verifies their government-issued identification matches the
    information they provided on the web form.

Step 3: Send documents to customer for signature with field data gathered from the customer
:   Docusign eSignature generates a document for your customer to sign. Fields in the document
    incorporate data gathered from the customer, generating a personalized and
    pre-filled document for their review and signature.

Step 4: Archive a copy of the completed agreement to your external storage system
:   The Google extension app saves a copy of the completed agreement in a Google Drive folder
    named for your workflow process.

Once you start the workflow, each step runs automatically at the right time and with
the right information.

title: Success Rules for ID Verification
text:
Read about how you can use branching rules to check the results of an identity
verification step.

If your workflow contains a Verify Someone's Identity step, you should include a true/false branching rule
to check whether the participant passed the identity verification. If they fail the
verification, you can use a branching rule to control what happens in the workflow.

If a participant fails the identity check, the workflow will still move to the next step.
To control the workflow progress, add a branching rule after the Verify Someone's Identity step to
check if they successfully validated their identity.

title: Success Rules for ID Verification
text:
Add a branching rule after the Verify Someone's Identity step. Configure the rule as
follows:

- For what to check, select the identity step and the variable
  `Validated`.
- For the operator, select `Is`.
- For the result, select `True`.

![Screenshot of the configuration for a branching rule to validate an identity verification step.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/validateidvstepconfig.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4NDUsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.6dyEOdbrKQf5haDEc17YQXOLLOGTqYWybUx17ut0LEQ&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the configuration for a branching rule to validate an identity verification step.")

title: Success Rules for ID Verification
heading_path: Add steps to the rule outcomes
text:
Depending on how you want your workflow to proceed after an identity step, add steps
to the True and False outcomes of the
rule.

- Continue your workflow steps on the True branch of the
  rule. These additional steps define the workflow process after the participant
  successfully passes the identity check.
- Add a confirmation step to the False branch. Configure it
  to display custom message explaining that the participant did not pass the
  identity verification.

![Screenshot of a set of workflow steps to validate the results of an identity verification step.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/idvvalidationbranchingrule.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4NDUsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.6dyEOdbrKQf5haDEc17YQXOLLOGTqYWybUx17ut0LEQ&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of a set of workflow steps to validate the results of an identity verification step.")

title: Configure a Verify Someone's Identity Step
heading_path: Configure a Verify Someone's Identity Step
text: Learn how to configure a Verify Someone's Identity step in an agreement workflow. You use an identity verification step to verify a participant's identity using Docusign's ID Verification process.

title: Configure a Verify Someone's Identity Step
heading_path: Configure a Verify Someone's Identity Step
text:
Before configuring a Verify Someone's Identity step, ensure you meet the following prerequisites:

- You know the name of the participants whose identity you want to verify at the point in your workflow where you add the step
- You’re using an ID Verification configuration that respects the following criteria:

  1. The configuration does not include the **Manually review the ID document** option
  2. The configuration does not include the **Allow SMS authentication after one successful Photo ID or Passport verification** option

You’re a process builder who wants to add a **Verify Someone's Identity** step to your agreement process. You’re adding this **Verify Someone's Identity** step for verifying participants’ identity and gathering identity data later in the workflow. You may consider using this step to secure access to envelopes. However, you do not want to use it only for that purpose.

Note: If your workflow requires ID verification only to access and sign agreements, use the Send Documents for Signature step. For more information, read the [Configure a Send Documents for Signature Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uww1698167288211.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to add and configure a workflow step to send a document for signature.").

If you add a Verify Someone's Identity step, you may need to record the verification details in ID Evidence. In this case, you must include a Store files step after the Verify someone's identity step in the workflow. Adding this step ensures that ID Evidence stores the identity verification data in a proof file within the designated archive folder.

1. Create a workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus "Learn the basics of how to create an agreement workflow. Process creators and account administrators can create workflows to automate the agreement process.").

   The workflow configuration begins. You can now add a new step.
2. Select Add a step >  Verify Someone's Identity > Configure.

   The configuration pane for your step opens.
3. Select the identification verification configuration your participants will use to identify themselves.

   Available flows include all supported identity verification configurations. For more information on supported configurations, read the [Supported identity verification methods](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uak1735246809132.html&anchor=uak1735246809132__section_jrd_ndj_vdc&_LANG=enus) page.
4. Select Next.
5. Select a participant for the step.

   The dropdown lists any pre-existing participants you have configured. To add a new participant, select or Add Participants.
6. Select a variable to map to the selected participant's name.

   You can map data from configured starting variables or from data gathered in any previous step, such as a web forms step. These are the fields that will be used to verify identity against the ID document fields or Knowledge-Based answers provided by the participant.

   Note: If the participant information was mapped in a prior step, this mapping is carried forward to the current step. You can change the mapping if needed.
7. Select Apply.

The verification step is now configured. You can view details from a completed verify identity step when monitoring an existing workflow. The step's Status Details lists key information for the identity verification activity. It includes the identification method, the time of the verification, and the result. See the [Monitor a Running Workflow Instance](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=vcp1698707326210.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to check the progress of the agreement instances you start from a workflow.") topic for information on monitoring a workflow process.

title: Configure a Verify Someone's Identity Step
heading_path: Configure a Verify Someone's Identity Step
text: If you add a Verify Someone's Identity step, consider including a Store files step after it to record the verification details. Adding this step allows ID Evidence to store the identity verification data in a proof file within the designated archive folder. For more information, read the [Capture ID Evidence Data](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=dar1749205503877.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to configure your workflow to enable ID Evidence to capture data.") page.

title: Pause a Published Workflow
text:
Learn how to pause a workflow or resume a paused workflow.

Pausing a published workflow allows all running instances to continue but no new instances can
be started. You can resume a paused workflow at any time to return it to a published
state. Pausing preserves the workflow URL. If you have embedded or distributed the
URL anywhere, it won't allow new instances to start. But when you resume the
workflow, the URL will once again allow new instances.

Paused workflows are included in your account's quota of active workflows. If you
want to unpublish a workflow to remove it from this quota, you must first pause it.

1. Log in to your Docusign account and select Agreements >  Workflows.

   The Workflows list shows all workflows that you
   have access to.
2. Locate the workflow that you want to pause. Filter by Status:
   Published and Created by: Me.
3. Select the workflow more actions menu and choose
   Pause New Instances.

   Your workflow status changes to "Paused". Any running instances will
   continue but no new instances can be started. Once all instances are complete or
   canceled, you can unpublish the workflow.
4. To unpause the workflow, select the more actions menu
   and choose Resume New Instances.

   Your workflow status changes back to "Published". The workflow is
   available to start new instances.

title: eSignature Requirements and Other Information
text:
Read about some important requirements to consider when configuring a Send Documents for Signature step.

Using a Send Documents for Signature step in a workflow involves certain requirements. This topic provides additional information to consider when configuring this step type.

title: eSignature Requirements and Other Information
text:
If your Send Documents for Signature step uses a template that specifies a SMS or WhatsApp notification method, your account settings must allow for these delivery methods. Your account administrator manages these settings in Admin >  Sending Settings >  Fields and Properties.

To use both SMS text message and email delivery methods together, your account administrator must also ensure the Allow SMS delivery to recipients user permission is enabled for your user profile. If you are unable to follow the steps in this topic, contact your account administrator to enable these options. Read more about these methods in the topic [Multi-Channel Delivery for Notifications: SMS and WhatsApp](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gav1643676262430&topicId=kwk1607393003979.html&_LANG=enus).

title: eSignature Requirements and Other Information
text:
By default, the first signer's experience is *direct signing*. This option takes them directly from a prior workflow step (such as a web form or ID Verification step) into the signing session. If you choose to turn off direct signing for your first signer, they will use the eSignature notification to access the signing session.

Note: Direct signing is not available for Docusign IAM accounts with a Life Sciences 21 CFR Part 11 module. These accounts must remote signing where all recipients receive a link to the envelope to comply with Part 11 requirements.

If there are multiple recipients on your template, the first signer can use direct signing; the rest use the eSignature notifications (email, SMS text message, or WhatsApp) to access their signing sessions.

title: eSignature Requirements and Other Information
text:
Envelope custom fields help you categorize sent envelopes for internal organization, tracking, and reporting. If your account has ECFs defined and visible to senders, these metadata are available in the Send Documents for Signature step. Process builders can specify values for text-type ECFs using workflow variables and select from the defined list of values for list-type ECFs.

**Important notes for ECFs**

- Any ECFs that are required must have a value provided in order to configure the step.
- Any ECF values specified on a template are not included.
- Only ECFs defined on your account are available.
- ECFs on imported templates are not included.
- ECFs defined on a template using APIs are not included.

title: eSignature Requirements and Other Information
text:
As an administrator, you can add signing brands that allow senders to include custom logos, color schemes, and company information on their envelopes.

By default, the system automatically evaluates available account, template, and workflow settings to determine which signing brand applies to your automation steps and signing sessions. Generally, a brand specified at the workflow level that is set to override a template's brand will be used. The only exception to this override is when the template's advanced settings are configured to restrict branding changes.

For more details and step-by-step instructions on how to apply a brand to your automation, see [Set Branding for a Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=task-6976.html&_LANG=enus "Choose a signing brand for your workflow to make sure all related emails and signing sessions use your custom logos and colors."). For instructions on creating or setting a default brand, refer to the help topic [Add a Signing Brand for Recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=byg1583277338658.html&_LANG=enus).

title: eSignature Requirements and Other Information
text:
Verifying the identity of a signer can be an important part of your use case. If this
is a requirement for you, use the identity verification option within your
eSignature template. Workflow Builder will honor the IDV requirements assigned to template
recipients. If phone authentication is specified, the process builder can map
variables to it in the eSignature configuration. Authentication results from
configured IDV methods are captured in the Certificate of Completion and in ID
Evidence.

If your workflow contains a Verify Someone's Identity and
Send Documents for Signature step with the identity verification
option, the system applies the identity claim generated from the first
Verify Someone's Identity step to confirm identity in the
Send Documents for Signature step. The Send Documents for Signature step uses the generated identity claim to validate
participants' identity and prevent them from verifying their identity again. The
Certificate of Completion also includes verification data from the Verify Someone's
Identity step. When you try to access the ID Evidence data
of the envelope, the system redirects you to the data of the Verify
Someone's Identity
 step.

If you want to use identity claims to verify the identity of your participants in a
Send Documents for Signature step, ensure that this step and the
Verify Someone's Identity step both use the same identity
verification method. For more information, read the [Identity Claims](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=vwg1731931235105.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about identity claims and how you can use them to streamline your automated agreement processes.") page.

title: eSignature Requirements and Other Information
heading_path: Check for envelope status with branching rules
text:
If your workflow includes a Send Documents for Signature step, you should
include branching rules to account for certain scenarios that lead to unplanned
envelope events. If your envelope is declined or voided by the sender, the workflow
will still continue unless you use a branching rule. For more information, see [Configuration Details for Complex Branching Rules](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=jaf1716918359288.html&_LANG=enus "Read about nesting IF statements and using advanced operators in a branching rule workflow step.")

title: eSignature Requirements and Other Information
text:
A confirmation step specifies what to show to a participant after they finish their
workflow task. In some cases, you will want to add a confirmation step after the
Send Documents for Signature step.

- If your template has only one recipient, add a confirmation step if the
  participant does not have any further tasks at that point in the workflow.
- If your template has multiple recipients and you use the direct signing option
  for the first recipient, a confirmation step is not necessary. The direct
  signing recipient is shown a system message indicating that they are done with
  their workflow tasks.

title: eSignature Requirements and Other Information
text:
For a Send Documents for Signature step, you use a prepared template or generated document to create an
envelope. As an option, you can map data fields from other sources to prepopulate
the document fields. This mapping is done in the Prepare eSignature Template or Generate Document step. This data can be basic strings, for values like text, dates,
and email addresses. You can also map data to checkbox, radio
group, and dropdown fields.

When you map data from a web form to fields in an eSignature template, an account-level Admin Sending Setting controls whether the mapped values are visible to all recipients at send time or only to the recipient assigned those fields. If the **When an envelope is sent, write the initial value of the field for all recipients** setting is turned off, mapped web form values only appear to the recipient who is assigned those fields until they finish signing.

For more information on the specific requirements for mapping web form field data, see [this help topic](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=cond11d1369-0918-4721-b25c-35149675b539.html&_LANG=enus "Learn the structural requirements and formatting rules for mapping advanced data types, such as checkboxes, radio groups, and dropdowns, from a web form to an agreement template.").

title: eSignature Requirements and Other Information
text:
A Send Documents for Signature step supports templates that contain Salesforce
merge fields. To use this feature, your account must have the Salesforce extension
app installed and an authorized connection to your Salesforce organization.

Provided these prerequisites are met, you can build a workflow that uses an envelope
template with Salesforce merge fields. Your workflow must contain a Read
from Salesforce step along with the Get
Signatures step. The Get Signatures step
configuration includes an additional step to select a source object for mapping data
to these fields.

For more detailed information on how to use a template with Salesforce merge fields
in a workflow, see [this help topic](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=jxv1738703407057.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Configure a Show a Confirmation Screen Step
heading_path: Configure a Show a Confirmation Screen Step
text:
Learn how to add a Show a Confirmation Screen step to your
workflow. This step generates a message screen that appears to a
specific workflow participant to convey information about their progress.

Use the Show a Confirmation
Screen step to show an onscreen message to a specific workflow
participant. A confirmation step generates a message screen for specific workflow participants. By supporting rich text formatting and workflow variables, these screens deliver visually engaging, dynamic content that clearly guides users through their next steps. Use one of the preconfigured messages or customize the message with your own content.

Confirmation steps are usually added to the end of the
participant's actions to inform them they are finished. They are
also used with branching rules to handle a failed state when the rule conditions are not met.
For information on branching rules, see [this
topic](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=mnv1698169910928.html&_LANG=enus "You can create conditional rules in your agreement workflow. These rules validate a condition and branch the workflow based on whether the condition is met or not.").

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/exampledeclinedrule.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MjQsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.HduR0JX1qkihiqF7bAW477u77mxM3ArZ36AR_scVsnA&_LANG=enus)

1. Create a new workflow as described in the topic [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus "Learn the basics of how to create an agreement workflow. Process creators and account administrators can create workflows to automate the agreement process.").
2. After a step where you want a confirmation message, select Add a step >  Show a Confirmation Screen >  Configure.

   The configuration panel for your step opens with the default message type
   selected. ![Screenshot of the configuration panel for a confirmation step.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/confirmationconfigurator.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MjQsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.HduR0JX1qkihiqF7bAW477u77mxM3ArZ36AR_scVsnA&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the configuration panel for a confirmation step.")
3. Select the Participant who will see the confirmation
   message.
4. Select the Message type you want to use. You can use one
   of the pre-built messages or write your own custom message.

   Use the rich text formatting and add workflow variables to create engaging, personalized content for your participant. Type "/" to select a variable. ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/message_with_variable.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MjQsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.HduR0JX1qkihiqF7bAW477u77mxM3ArZ36AR_scVsnA&_LANG=enus)
5. Select Apply to save your configuration.

Your Show a Confirmation Screen step is
configured. When a participant reaches this step in your workflow, your message will
display on their screen.

title: Configure an Export Data to OneDrive Step﻿
heading_path: Configure an Export Data to OneDrive Step﻿
text:
Learn how to configure a workflow step to export data in a tabular format to OneDrive.

You can export variables, including eSignature and web form data, to tabular format files in OneDrive to analyze and extract meaningful insights. Use the **Export Data to OneDrive** step to select and export these variables.

The following file formats are supported for data export:

- CSV
- MS Excel

Important:

The **Export Data to OneDrive** step only supports export of **string-type** workflow variables to OneDrive.

title: Configure an Export Data to OneDrive Step﻿
heading_path: Configure an Export Data to OneDrive Step﻿
text:
- You must have [installed and authorized](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ctg1698170340729.html&_LANG=enus) the OneDrive extension app on your account.
- You must be an account administrator or have Workflows: Create user permissions as described in the [Workflow Builder Roles and Permission Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the different roles involved with creating and using workflows with Docusign Workflow Builder. Each role requires specific user permissions.") article.
- You must have a supported file type (CSV or MS Excel) saved on your configured OneDrive.

1. Create a workflow as described in the [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus "Learn the basics of how to create an agreement workflow. Process creators and account administrators can create workflows to automate the agreement process.") topic.
2. Add the required step to provide data to export to OneDrive (for example, **Collect Data with Web Forms** step or **Send Documents for Signature** step).
3. Select Add a step > Export Data to OneDrive > Configure.

   The configuration panel for the Export Data to OneDrive step opens.
4. Select where to store the data:
   1. Select the existing connection from the Select connection dropdown list where the destination tabular sheet (CSV or MS Excel) exists.

      Note: If the required OneDrive connection is not available in the dropdown list, create the connection using the Add New Connection button.
   2. Select Choose File.

      ![Screenshot to select the required connection and file to export data.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/maestro-onedrive-choosefile.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MTMsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.E912hLgiS34pi9pSmHbAZYFfUi01MeL3Cekzod8qMvQ&_LANG=enus "Screenshot to select the required connection and file to export data.")

      The Select files from OneDrive dialog box opens.

      Note: All available files connected to the Microsoft account display.
   3. Go to the file to use for the export and select the checkbox.
   4. Select Submit.
   5. Select Next.
5. Select an export method to export data variables.

   ![Screenshot to select the required export method and variables to export data.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/maestro-onedrive-addvariables.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MTMsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.E912hLgiS34pi9pSmHbAZYFfUi01MeL3Cekzod8qMvQ&_LANG=enus "Screenshot to select the required export method and variables to export data.")

   - [**Bulk Export**]: Use this method to export all envelope metadata to the tabular format:
     1. Select Bulk export.
     2. Select Done.
     3. Select a data source for the export.
     4. Choose the Envelope Info variable to use from the dropdown list.

        Note: Any envelope generated by earlier workflow steps is available here to select.
   - [**Specific Export**]: Use this method to export specific workflow variables to the tabular format:
     1. Select Specific export.
     2. Select Done.
     3. Select the Add variable button.
     4. Select the Search for variable or step dropdown list to select the required data variables.
     5. Repeat steps **c** and **d** to add more variables.

title: Configure an Export Data to OneDrive Step﻿
heading_path: Configure an Export Data to OneDrive Step﻿
text:
3. Select the Add variable button.
     4. Select the Search for variable or step dropdown list to select the required data variables.
     5. Repeat steps **c** and **d** to add more variables.

   Important: To ensure that data is exported correctly, the column
   headers in the destination file must exactly match the names of your selected data fields. If a
   match is not found, the system will create a new column for that data.
   Columns in the destination file that are not configured for export will be
   left blank.
6. Select Apply.

You have now configured an Export Data to OneDrive step in a workflow.

title: Test a Web Request Configuration
text:
Confirms that a configured Make a Web Request step successfully communicates with an external system and returns the expected data.

Use this procedure after you configure a Make a Web Request step to confirm that the step can successfully call the external system and return the data you expect.

title: Test a Web Request Configuration
text:
Before you begin, make sure:

- You have a published workflow with the web request step. The step is configured with a connection or endpoint that is appropriate for testing. For example, a sandbox connection.
- You understand what a successful response looks like (for example, a specific status code or field value in the response).

1. Start a test run of the workflow.
   1. From Workflow Builder, start a new instance using test data that exercises the Web Request step.
   2. If your workflow collects participant input, enter realistic but non-production values.
2. Monitor the Web Request step during the run.
   1. Open the workflow run details.
   2. Locate the Web Request step in the run timeline.
   3. Check whether the step shows a successful status or an error.
3. If the step succeeds, verify the response in Workflow Builder.
   1. Confirm that the response variables you mapped in the Web Request step are populated.
   2. Check that any downstream steps that use those variables behave as expected (for example, the workflow follows the correct branch based on a risk score or status value).
   3. If needed, run an additional test with different inputs to confirm that the workflow reacts correctly to different responses.
4. If the step fails, review the error and make basic corrections.
   1. Read any error message or status code shown for the Web Request step.
   2. Check for common configuration issues:

      - The URL is misspelled or missing required path segments.
      - The connection is pointing to the wrong environment (for example, production instead of sandbox).
      - Required headers or body fields are missing or use the wrong data type.
   3. Update the Web Request step configuration as needed, then save the workflow and run another test.
5. (Optional) Confirm the external system was updated correctly.
   1. If the Web Request is supposed to create or update data in another system, sign in to that system or use its logs to confirm that the call arrived and was processed as expected.
   2. If the external system does not show the expected change, share the workflow run details and any error messages with your technical team so they can check the API configuration.

When you are satisfied with the test results:

- Keep the test configuration if it matches your production requirements, or switch the connection or endpoint to the production values your technical team has approved.
- Save the workflow and follow your normal process for enabling or publishing it for broader use.

title: Set Branding for a Workflow
text:
Choose a signing brand for your workflow to make sure all related emails and signing sessions use your custom logos and colors.

Workflow Builder uses the signing brands configured on your account to control the visual appearance of workflow emails and signing sessions. This branding includes logos, header color, and button color. You set branding at the workflow level from the Workflow Builder designer, rather than configuring it separately on each step.

You can choose to replace the branding on your templates with your workflow branding. However, if the eSignature template is configured to restrict branding changes, the template's original brand will always take precedence. If your chosen brand fails to load for some reason, Workflow Builder will automatically use your account's default look. If that isn't available either, it will fall back to standard Docusign branding.

title: Set Branding for a Workflow
text:
- Your admin has created one or more signing brands for your account (Admin > Brands > Signing).
- You have permission to edit and publish the workflow.

1. Open your workflow in Workflow Builder.
2. At the top of the screen, select More (...) > Branding.
3. Select the signing brand that you want to use.

   The preview updates to show the signing and email logos and the header and button colors for the selected brand.
4. (Optional) check the box for Override custom template branding if you want this brand to replace the branding defined on templates used in this workflow.
5. Review the preview to confirm that the logos and colors look correct.
6. Select Apply.
7. Publish or republish the workflow so the branding changes take effect.

The workflow uses the selected signing brand for its emails and signing sessions, subject to any template-level brand locks and fallback behavior.

title: Set Branding for a Workflow
text:
Important:

If an eSignature template owner or administrator has disabled Allow senders to edit the logo and brand colors in the template’s Advanced Options, that template’s branding is locked and cannot be changed by the workflow branding. This restriction persists even if you select Override custom template branding.

title: Configure a Create Request Step
text:
Learn how to add a Create Request step to a workflow to generate automatically or interactively a new Agreement Desk request.

You use a **Create Request** step to initiate an Agreement Desk request directly from your workflow. This step creates a complete, ready-to-work request package by pulling in data and documents generated in earlier workflow steps or passed through API calls.

**Execution Modes:** You can configure this step to run in one of two modes:

- **Automatic:** The system generates the request automatically and unattended, making it ideal for high-volume, zero-touch workflows. Data for the request is mapped directly from previous workflow steps or starting variables.

  Note: To support external participants (such as outside vendors or customers), you can place a Collect Data with Web Form step prior to the Create Request step and map the captured form fields directly into Automatic mode.
- **Interactive:** A designated internal participant manually completes the request form. This mode is designed for CRM integrations. Internal teams can initiate and route requests directly from their existing workflows.

**Example use case (Automated Contract Renewal):** You can set up an automated workflow triggered by an API call. When a contract is 30 days from expiring, your CRM triggers the workflow, passing data into a **Create Request** step running in Automatic mode. The system silently generates a renewal request for your team without any manual intervention.

**Example use case (High-Value Contract Approval):** You can build an internal web form using Workflow Builder to collect contract data from your team. If a submitted contract's value exceeds $50,000, the workflow automatically creates an Agreement Desk request and routes it to the appropriate VP for review and approval.

Important:

This step is **not available** for workflows that use the From an Agreement Desk request start method.

title: Configure a Create Request Step
text:
- Ensure **Agreement Desk** is enabled and active for your organization's Docusign account.
- Create a workflow with a supported start method (such as From a Link, From an Event, From an API Call, or Manually in Docusign).

1. In the workflow designer, select **Add a step**.
2. Select **Create Request** > **Configure**.
3. Select the **request type** for the new request from the list.

   Note: This list displays the custom request types configured in your organization's Agreement Desk account.
4. Select how the form will be filled out: **Automatic** or **Interactive**.
5. Construct the **Request title** using manual text, up to five workflow variables, or a combination of both.
6. Map workflow variables to the request form fields.

   - *Automatic mode:* Mapped values cannot be changed by a user. If required fields from the request type are left unmapped, the workflow will ignore the requirement, leave the fields blank, and still successfully create the request.
   - *Interactive mode:* The assigned participant can manually change any mapped values when completing the form.
7. Configure **Documents** by choosing how to pull documents into the request.

   - Include all workflow documents.
   - Manually select specific documents to include.
   - Do not include any documents.

   *Note:* As part of the mapping experience, you can also map specific workflow documents directly to attachment fields in the request form.
8. Select **Apply**.

Your **Create Request** step is configured.

title: Configure an Export Data to SharePoint Step
heading_path: Configure an Export Data to SharePoint Step
text:
Learn how to configure a workflow step to export data in a tabular format to SharePoint.

You can export variables, including eSignature and web form data, to tabular format files in SharePoint to analyze and extract meaningful insights. Use the **Export Data to SharePoint** step to select and export these variables.

The following file formats are supported for data export:

- CSV
- MS Excel

Important:

The **Export Data to SharePoint** step only supports export of **string-type** workflow variables to SharePoint.

title: Configure an Export Data to SharePoint Step
heading_path: Configure an Export Data to SharePoint Step
text:
- You must have [installed and authorized](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ctg1698170340729.html&_LANG=enus) the SharePoint extension app on your account.
- You must be an account administrator or have Workflows: Create user permissions as described in the [Workflow Builder Roles and Permission Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the different roles involved with creating and using workflows with Docusign Workflow Builder. Each role requires specific user permissions.") article.
- You must have a supported file type (CSV or MS Excel) saved on your configured SharePoint account.

1. Create a workflow as described in the [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus "Learn the basics of how to create an agreement workflow. Process creators and account administrators can create workflows to automate the agreement process.") topic.
2. Add the required step to provide data to export to SharePoint (for example, **Collect Data with Web Forms** step or **Send Documents for Signature** step).
3. Select Add a step > Export Data to SharePoint > Configure.

   The configuration panel for the Export Data to SharePoint step opens.
4. Select where to store the data:
   1. Select the existing connection from the Select connection dropdown list where the destination tabular sheet (CSV or MS Excel) exists.

      Note: If the required SharePoint connection is not available in the dropdown list, create the connection using the Add New Connection button.
   2. Select Choose File.

      ![Screenshot to select the required connection and file to export data.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/maestro-sharepoint-choosefile.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MDgsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.tUq8w7zGtsUYvNsCu89DIbMYFd43SHM8_VVaGXuB_RE&_LANG=enus "Screenshot to select the required connection and file to export data.")

      The Select files from SharePoint dialog box opens.

      Note: All available sites and drives connected to the SharePoint account display.
   3. Select the required SharePoint site and select Next.
   4. Select the required drive and select Next.

      Note: The drive is also referred to as **Document Library** in SharePoint.
   5. Go to the file to use for the export and select the checkbox.
   6. Select Next.
5. Select an export method to export data variables.

   ![Screenshot to select the required export method and variables to export data.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/maestro-sharepoint-addvariables.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MDgsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.tUq8w7zGtsUYvNsCu89DIbMYFd43SHM8_VVaGXuB_RE&_LANG=enus "Screenshot to select the required export method and variables to export data.")

   - [**Bulk Export**]: Use this method to export all envelope metadata to the tabular format:
     1. Select Bulk export.
     2. Select Done.
     3. Select a data source for the export.
     4. Choose the Envelope Info variable to use from the dropdown list.

        Note: Any envelope generated by earlier workflow steps is available here to select.
   - [**Specific Export**]: Use this method to export specific workflow variables to the tabular format:
     1. Select Specific export.
     2. Select Done.
     3. Select the Add variable button.
     4. Select the Search for variable or step dropdown list to select the required data variables.
     5. Repeat steps **c** and **d** to add more variables.

title: Configure an Export Data to SharePoint Step
heading_path: Configure an Export Data to SharePoint Step
text:
3. Select the Add variable button.
     4. Select the Search for variable or step dropdown list to select the required data variables.
     5. Repeat steps **c** and **d** to add more variables.

   Important: To ensure that data is exported correctly, the column
   headers in the destination file must exactly match the names of your selected data fields. If a
   match is not found, the system will create a new column for that data.
   Columns in the destination file that are not configured for export will be
   left blank.
6. Select Apply.

You have now configured an Export Data to SharePoint step in a workflow.

title: Configure a Make a Web Request Step
heading_path: Configure a Make a Web Request Step
text:
Use a Make a Web Request step to call an external or internal service from Workflow Builder to look up data or push an update without a dedicated app.

Use a Make a Web Request step when you want Workflow Builder to call another system in the middle of a process (for example, to look up data or push an update). Follow these steps to configure the Web Request step.

title: Configure a Make a Web Request Step
heading_path: Configure a Make a Web Request Step
text:
Confirm that you have access to the HTTP Web Request feature:

- Your account includes access to the [HTTP web request extension app](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ijy1698170251179.html). This app is automatically installed for Docusign IAM premium accounts.
- An administrator or technical owner has created at least one Web Request connection for the target API. Currently, Basic Auth connections are supported (username and password). The connection provides valid credentials to authenticate access to the target API.
- You know which endpoint, method, and data your technical team expects you to use.

1. Open your workflow in Workflow Builder.
2. On the canvas, select the plus icon (`+`) where you want to add the step.

   Add the step where the external call should occur (for example, after collecting participant data and before sending an envelope).
3. In the step selector, choose the Make a Web Request step from the Developer section.

   The step configurator opens for you to define the request details.
4. Select the connection to use for the request.

   - If more than one connection is available, choose the option that matches the target system (for example, "Risk Scoring API – Production" or "CLM API – Sandbox").
   - If you do not see the connection that you need, contact your administrator to create or share it.

   Note: The connection controls how Workflow Builder authenticates with the external service. You do not need to enter credentials directly in the step.
5. Define the destination method and endpoint URL.
   1. Choose the HTTP method:

      - GET to retrieve data from the external system.
      - POST to send new data or trigger an action.
      - PUT to update existing data.
   2. Enter the endpoint URL that the web request will access.
6. Define the request by configuring a JSON payload and passing additional metadata about your request.

   If you selected POST or PUT and need to send a request body, configure the body of the request:

   1. Enter the JSON input provided by your technical team.
   2. Use the variable picker to insert workflow variables as values for a key/value pair into the payload so the request includes participant input or other step data. Enter one variable per line.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/JSON_input_sample.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4NTgsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.9J5fVbnqiOk2Djd-aK__TAZvgm0XtzkYJC4TYczGC-I&_LANG=enus)

      Tip: Quotation marks are not required for variable references. The system automatically wraps variables in quotation marks at runtime if they are needed.
   3. Confirm that the data types (for example, numbers, dates, booleans) match what the API expects.
7. (Optional) Configure any additional headers that your API requires.

   Define custom HTTP headers as key-value pairs to provide necessary metadata required by the destination API. You can add a maximum of 10 headers per request.

   1. In the Headers area, add a row for each header.
   2. Enter the header key (name). Limit: 256 characters.
   3. Enter a static value (manual entry) or map a workflow variable into the value if the header should change per request. Limit: 4,000 characters.

      Important: The Authorization header is automatically populated at runtime with the values from the connection selected for the step.
8. (Optional) Configure query parameters.

   Use query parameters and key-value pairs to filter, sort, or search for specific data at the destination endpoint without needing to manually edit the base endpoint URL string. You can add a maximum of 10 query parameters per request.

title: Configure a Make a Web Request Step
heading_path: Configure a Make a Web Request Step
text:
pairs to filter, sort, or search for specific data at the destination endpoint without needing to manually edit the base endpoint URL string. You can add a maximum of 10 query parameters per request.

   1. In the Query Parameters area, add a row for each parameter.
   2. Enter the parameter key (name). Limit: 256 characters.
   3. Enter the parameter value. You can enter a static text value (manual entry) or use the variable picker to insert workflow variables (such as an account ID or envelope ID) so the request is tailored dynamically to each workflow run. Limit: 4,000 characters.

      Note: The configurator automatically URL encodes the values that you enter. This ensures that any spaces or special characters passed by variables do not cause the HTTP request to fail.
9. Define the output variables for the response data.

   For GET requests, generate response variables or enter the response manually and map these to workflow variables. This way, later steps can use the data returned by the external system

   1. Select Run Request.

      The Code Editor opens with the request response. If the request couldn’t run, you can enter a sample response manually and select Regenerate to generate response variables.
   2. Map data types to variables.

      For each generated variable, review and confirm the data type selected is accurate. Edit the data type as needed.
   3. Select Save and Close.
10. Select Apply.

    Your Make a Web Request step is configured.

title: Creating Workflows
text:
Learn how process builders plan, prepare, and structure end-to-end agreement automation using the Workflow Builder design canvas.

This section presents information on how process builders can create agreement workflows. Start
by reviewing [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus "Learn the basics of how to create an agreement workflow. Process creators and account administrators can create workflows to automate the agreement process."). Once you have an idea of the kind of
workflow you want to create, gather the following information to prepare:

title: Creating Workflows
text:
ors and account administrators can create workflows to automate the agreement process."). Once you have an idea of the kind of
workflow you want to create, gather the following information to prepare:

- How will your workflow be initiated? Manually by someone on your account? Or through an
  external system that will pass data into the process? Reference [Workflow Start Methods](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=ztb1727892686033.html&_LANG=enus "Review the different automated and manual trigger methods available to initiate processes in Workflow Builder, including links, events, API calls, and CLM integrations."), [Enhance CLM Workflows with Workflow Builder Automation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=hmz1727408977389.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how process builders trigger Workflow Builder processes from Docusign CLM to access advanced delivery options and signature features.")
- Who are the participants who will take part in your agreement process? Reference
  [Define Workflow Participants](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=ypt1698264964168.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to define, configure, and manage participant roles within your workflow steps to control how users interact with your agreement automation.")
- Do you want to gather data from participants to use in the agreement process? What
  stand-alone web form will you use? Reference [Configure a Collect Data with Web Forms Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gua1698120920620.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to configure a Collect Data with Web Forms step in an agreement workflow. You use a web forms step to gather information from participants. You can use any existing stand-alone web form on your account that you have access to for your step.")
- Do you need to bring participants into the workflow after it starts by sending an invitation?
  Reference [Configure a Set Up Invite Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=kfx1698167344720.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use a Set Up Invite step in your agreement workflow to invite participants to join the workflow.")
- Will you have a participant run an identity verification check? Reference [Configure a Verify Someone's Identity Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=qht1698126463108.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to configure a Verify Someone's Identity step in an agreement workflow. You use an identity verification step to verify a participant's identity using Docusign's ID Verification process.")
- What documents do you want to use in the agreement process? Reference [Configure a Prepare Document Template Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=cra1749229692878.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to incorporate a document template into your process to generate customized agreements automatically using workflow variables.") and [Configure a Prepare eSignature Template Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=tskd83cc960-fb4b-4534-9573-1041591501e2.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to incorporate an eSignature template into your process, prefill recipient fields using upstream variables, and route completed documents to a secure repository.")
- Which participants will receive and sign your documents? Reference [Configure a Send Documents for Signature Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uww1698167288211.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to add and configure a workflow step to send a document for signature.")
- Do you want to use a branching rule in your workflow to adjust for different scenarios? Reference [Configure a Branching Rule](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=mnv1698169910928.html&_LANG=enus "You can create conditional rules in your agreement workflow. These rules validate a condition and branch the workflow based on whether the condition is met or not.")

title: Creating Workflows
text: Here's a short video to help you understand how to create agreement workflows.

title: Workflow Utility Steps
text:
This section contains detailed configuration information for functional steps in the agreement process.

[Configure a Set Up Invite Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=kfx1698167344720.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use a Set Up Invite step in your agreement workflow to invite participants to join the workflow.")
:   Set up and manage steps to invite participants to join the workflow. Inviting participants is required for them to receive the necessary information and access to participate.

[Configure a Show a Confirmation Screen Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=rpc1698682081448.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to add a Show a Confirmation Screen step to your workflow. This step generates a message screen that appears to a specific workflow participant to convey information about their progress.")
:   Design and configure steps to confirm actions or decisions made by participants. Confirmations provide clear acknowledgment and record of their input.

[Configure a Send an Email Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=ker1716504515479.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to add an email notification step to your agreement workflow. This step generates an ad hoc email notification to convey information to any workflow participant or other named individual.")
:   Set up automated email notifications to keep participants informed at various stages of the workflow. Notifications enhance communication and transparency.

[Configure a Branching Rule](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=mnv1698169910928.html&_LANG=enus "You can create conditional rules in your agreement workflow. These rules validate a condition and branch the workflow based on whether the condition is met or not.")
:   Implement branching rules to direct the workflow based on specific conditions or participant responses. Branching provides dynamic and adaptable workflow paths.

[Configure a Multi Branch Rule](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=xtg1749585007265.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use the Add a Multi Branch Rule step in Docusign Workflow Builder to create workflows with multiple paths based on specific conditions. This feature allows for dynamic processes that adapt based on data collected from web forms.")
:   Add flexibility to your workflow with a multi branch rule that can contain up to 15 different paths.

[Configure an Add Parallel Branches Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=tsk9f5912a8-48d1-4433-9408-f0c6c9470c21.html&_LANG=enus "Use the Add Parallel Branches step to split a single workflow path into multiple branches that run at the same time.")
:   Run simultaneous tasks with up to 15 parallel branch paths.

[Connect to Any Service Using Web Requests](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=conbf387387-0435-452a-a7bb-fd1f719980eb.html&_LANG=enus "Docusign Workflow Builder allows you to trigger external APIs using the Make a Web Request utility step.")
:   Send HTTP requests (GET, POST, or PUT) to almost any external or internal service.

title: Configure an Export Data to Google Drive Step
heading_path: Configure an Export Data to Google Drive Step
text: Learn how to configure a workflow step to export data in a tabular format to Google Drive.

title: Configure an Export Data to Google Drive Step
heading_path: Configure an Export Data to Google Drive Step
text:
- You must have [installed and authorized](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ctg1698170340729.html&_LANG=enus) the Google Drive extension app on your account.
- You must be an account administrator or have Workflows: Create user permissions as described in the [Workflow Builder Roles and Permission Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the different roles involved with creating and using workflows with Docusign Workflow Builder. Each role requires specific user permissions.") article.
- You must have a supported file type (Google Sheets, CSV, or MS Excel) saved on your configured Google Drive.

You can export variables, including eSignature and web form data,
to tabular format files in Google Drive. This helps users to analyze and extract
meaningful insights from the data. Use the **Export Data to
Google Drive** step to select and export these variables.

The following file formats are supported for data export:

- Google Sheets
- CSV
- MS Excel

Important: The Google Drive app is optimized for Google Sheets file
formats. When you export large volumes of data to CSV or MS Excel file formats,
there may be a delay in writing data to the file. If this impacts your workflow,
Docusign recommends you to convert your CSV or MS Excel
format to Google Sheets.

Important: The **Export Data to Google Drive** step only supports export of **string-type** workflow variables to Google Drive.

.

1. Create a workflow as described in the [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus "Learn the basics of how to create an agreement workflow. Process creators and account administrators can create workflows to automate the agreement process.") topic.
2. Add the required step to provide data to export to Google Drive (for example, **Collect Data with Web Forms** step or **Send Documents for Signature** step).
3. Select Add a step > Export Data to Google Drive > Configure.

   The configuration panel for the Export Data to Google Drive step opens.
4. Select where to store the data:
   1. Select the existing connection from the Select connection dropdown list where the destination tabular sheet (Google Sheets, CSV or MS Excel) exists.

      Note: If the required GoogleDrive connection is not available in the dropdown list, create the connection using the Add New Connection button.
   2. Select Choose File.

      ![Screenshot to select the required connection and file to export data.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/maestro-googledrive-choosefile.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MDUsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.Albos7izuYhILeXtf1SxS6vuGeG-8RPO1n995MstsH0&_LANG=enus "Screenshot to select the required connection and file to export data.")

      The Select files from Google Drive dialog box opens.

      Note: All
      available drives are connected to the Google account
      display.
   3. Select the required drive and select Next.
   4. Go to the file to use for the export and select the checkbox.
   5. Select Submit.
   6. Select Next.
5. Select an export method to export data variables.

   ![Screenshot to select the required export method and variables to export data.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/maestro-googledrive-addvariables.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MDUsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.Albos7izuYhILeXtf1SxS6vuGeG-8RPO1n995MstsH0&_LANG=enus "Screenshot to select the required export method and variables to export data.")

   - [**Bulk Export**]: Use this method to export all envelope metadata to the tabular format:
     1. Select Bulk export.

     3. Select a data source for the export.

title: Create a Workflow from a Web Form
heading_path: Create a Workflow from a Web Form
text: Learn how to quickly convert an existing Docusign web form and its linked eSignature template into a fully configured Workflow Builder process in a single step, saving significant setup time.

title: Create a Workflow from a Web Form
heading_path: Create a Workflow from a Web Form
text:
- You must have access to a configured web form that is linked to an eSignature template. (The web form doesn't need to be active to use it it to create a workflow.)
- Creating workflows requires specific user permissions. For more information on required permissions to complete this task, see [Workflow Builder Roles and Permission Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the different roles involved with creating and using workflows with Docusign Workflow Builder. Each role requires specific user permissions.").

This topic explains how to create a Workflow Builder workflow directly from an existing web form. This feature allows you to quickly convert an existing Docusign Web Form and its linked eSignature template into a fully configured Workflow Builder workflow in a single step, saving significant setup time.

As a process builder, you can start a new workflow from a web form from various eSignature locations: from the main action menus of the Templates, Home, and Agreements pages, or from the Workflow Builder Workflows view.

When you create a workflow from a web form, the system automatically creates new workflow versions of the web form and its linked eSignature template to use in the workflow. The new web form is added to your list of web forms, named "Workflow Copy - [original web form name]", and activated. The web form is stand-alone and is not associated with any template. The workflow you create will have the following structure:

- The workflow's start method will be set to From a Link.
- Recipients from the template are automatically added to the workflow's list of participants.
- The workflow will include three main process steps: a Collect Data with Web Forms, Prepare eSignature Template, and Send Documents for Signature.
- The first signer role on the template is the participant role assigned to the web forms step.
- The workflow ends with a Show Confirmation Screen assigned to the participant assigned to the web forms step.

1. In your Docusign account, start the workflow creation process using one of the following methods:

   - From the Templates page: Select one of the WEB FORMS views from the left navigation. Locate the form you want to use, select the more actions menu, and choose Add Automation with Workflow Builder.
   - From the Home page: Select Create a Workflow > Start from Web Form. Select the form you want to use and click Continue.
   - From the Workflow Builder Workflows view on the Agreements page: Select Create Workflow > Start from Web Form. Select the web form you want to use and click Continue.

   The Preparing your workflow... message displays while your new workflow is created.
2. Review the steps of your new workflow. All steps are configured with participant and field mapping details.

title: Create a Workflow from a Web Form
heading_path: Create a Workflow from a Web Form
text:
.

   The Preparing your workflow... message displays while your new workflow is created.
2. Review the steps of your new workflow. All steps are configured with participant and field mapping details.

   - Participant information is created from the eSignature template recipient roles.
   - The Collect Data with Web Forms step uses the new workflow version of your web form. The first recipient role from the template is assigned as the workflow participant.
   - The Prepare eSignature Template step uses the new workflow version of your template. Web form data is mapped to fields in the eSignature template.
   - The Send Documents for Signature step uses the prepared eSignature template. Participant information is configured for all template recipient roles.![Screenshot of a workflow created from a web form.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/example_wf_from_web_form.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU2OTksInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.F1MutoMaOLjTVGeK-CWoNJCJRW6Ayth65Bvfl_SjtvQ&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of a workflow created from a web form.")
3. You can now publish the workflow as is, or continue to add more workflow steps to meet your process needs as described in the article [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus "Learn the basics of how to create an agreement workflow. Process creators and account administrators can create workflows to automate the agreement process.").

title: Access Rule Logs for Agreement Rules
heading_path: Access Rule Logs for Agreement Rules
text: Learn how to access execution logs for agreement rules used to trigger a Workflow Builder process.

title: Access Rule Logs for Agreement Rules
heading_path: Access Rule Logs for Agreement Rules
text:
Ensure you have access to the Agreement Rules list and the necessary permissions to view logs.

When you create a workflow from an agreement rule, the Workflow Builder Activity Logs contain information for the workflow activities. Since the workflow starts from a completed envelope, the Workflow Builder logs won't have details about the envelope.

You can find activity logs pertaining to a workflow-powered rule in the Agreement Rules list. Workflow Builder rule logs are stored separately from other rule logs. Logs for these rules are available through the Action menu for the rule. (Other rules use the Logs tab to record and access activity information.)

1. In Admin > Agreement Actions  > Rules, locate the rule with the "Powered by Workflow Builder" badge.
2. From the Rules tab, select **Action** > **View Instance Logs**.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/view_instance_logs.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU3MTMsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.KH-vWo3Qiv7Q7Sa1sWoJSaR_gNUW9YTvPJ8BN_JOIME&_LANG=enus)
3. Review the list of envelopes using the rule, which provides status and the event date and time of the rule execution.
4. Select **View Details** for complete information on the envelope, including action type, status, and other details.

title: Configure a DS Sender Profile for Workflow Builder Preparer Permissions
heading_path: Configure a DS Sender Profile for Workflow Builder Preparer Permissions
text:
Learn how to configure a custom Docusign DS Sender permission profile to grant users Workflow Builder preparer access.

Docusign uses permission profiles to control which actions users are allowed to perform. The default DS Sender profile allows users to view and send envelopes, use templates, and other actions. To enable Workflow Builder functionality for a sender, you need to create a custom permission profile based on DS Sender and add the **Workflows: Prepare** permission. You also need to assign your users the permission profile. The following steps show an example of how to enable Workflow Builder functionality for a new Docusign sender profile.

1. Log in as a Docusign Administrator. You must have administrative access to your Docusign account to manage permission profiles and user settings.
2. Select the Admin tab, and select Permission Profiles in the left navigation menu.

   ![The screenshot shows the Permissions Profiles option in the left navigation menu for administrators.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/ds-sender1.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU2NDAsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.jCdvlU96R2HnQHuVKP_70SBxXirQcbYoDFPNAg_XUPc&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Permissions Profiles option in the left navigation menu for administrators.")
3. Locate the DS Sender profile, and selectActions > Copy.

   ![The screenshot shows the Actions menu where you can copy the DS Sender permission profile.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/ds-sender2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU2NDAsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.jCdvlU96R2HnQHuVKP_70SBxXirQcbYoDFPNAg_XUPc&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Actions menu where you can copy the DS Sender permission profile.")

   The Copy Permission Profile dialog box opens.
4. Enter a descriptive name for the new permission profile, for example, "DS Sender with Workflow Access" or "Workflow Preparer Profile."
5. Select the User Permissions tab.
6. Scroll down to the  Workflows setting, and select Prepare.

   ![The screenshot shows the Workflows setting in the User Permissions options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/copy_permission_profile.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU2NDAsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.jCdvlU96R2HnQHuVKP_70SBxXirQcbYoDFPNAg_XUPc&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Workflows setting in the User Permissions options.")
7. Select Add to create this permission profile.
8. Select Users in the left navigation menu.
9. Find the user that you want to enable Workflow Builder for.
10. Select Actions > Edit from the dropdown menu.
11. In the Permission Profile dropdown, select the new permission profile that you created for Workflow Builder DS Sender or the descriptive name you applied.
12. Select Save.

The new permission profile using Workflow Builder is applied to the user. Assign the new permission profile to any other users that need preparer access to Workflow Builder.

title: Automate Agreement Desk Requests with Workflow Builder
heading_path: Automate Agreement Desk Requests with Workflow Builder
text:
Learn how to streamline the agreement lifecycle by using Workflow Builder to triage, manage, and automate incoming Agreement Desk requests.

By combining Agreement Desk triggers and actions inside Workflow Builder, you can streamline your contract lifecycle from initial intake to final signature. Use these automated processes to manage incoming requests, route approvals, assign ownership, and generate supplemental documents.

This topic provides the basic process for creating a workflow to automate many Agreement Desk request actions. For detailed guidance, including configuration details for the different Agreement Desk steps, see our guide, [Automating Agreement Desk Requests with Docusign Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map4317ef56-de36-43e2-9921-c76a445c3276&topicId=con27b1a75a-da95-4952-94a4-a20c498bf81c.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Define the Workflow Start Method.

   Configure your workflow to initiate automatically based on specific Agreement Desk events using the From an Agreement Desk request start method. This method includes triggers such as when a request is submitted, its type or status changes, an envelope is sent, or a signature is completed.![Screenshot showing the configuration options for the 'From an Agreement Desk request' start method in a workflow.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/start_method.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU2NzYsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.v6jcKvUUSalxp_JHDwxntTfkBYI7hk1-hRcrkc8GH1Y&_LANG=enus "Screenshot showing the configuration options for the 'From an Agreement Desk request' start method in a workflow.")
2. Add Agreement Desk Steps to your workflow.

   Incorporate a variety of Agreement Desk-specific steps into your workflow to manage requests efficiently. These steps allow you to:

   - Assign an owner to a request.
   - Add followers for visibility.
   - Change the request status.
   - Set a due date for the request.
   - Add permissions for users or groups.
   - Create internal approval requests.
3. Generate Documents for a Request.

   Use the **Prepare Document Template** step to generate customized documents within your workflow. These documents are then automatically added to the Agreement Desk request and can be sent for signature.
4. Collect a Request Document.

   Use the **Get Document from a Request** to collect the latest version of a request document to send for signature or archive to a system of records.
5. Implement Branching Rules.

   Use branching rules to introduce conditional logic, allowing your workflow to adapt dynamically based on outcomes such as approvals or the status of signature requests. This ensures that the process only moves forward when specific conditions are met.
6. Send documents for signature or to a system of record.

   Use standard Workflow Builder steps to send generated and collected documents for signature or store in Salesforce, Google Drive, or other systems of record. As an option, this step supports automated sending from the request submitter. See [Sender Impersonation and Consent for Agreement Desk Workflows](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map4317ef56-de36-43e2-9921-c76a445c3276&topicId=con85f610e6-2584-423e-840e-8823eb1ed065.html&_LANG=enus) for more information.

The workflow will run when the specified Agreement Desk event occurs with the conditions you selected. Your workflow steps automate key actions, streamlining and simplifying the request process.

title: Store Envelope Files to Third-Party Storage from an Event
heading_path: Store Envelope Files to Third-Party Storage from an Event
text:
Learn how to configure a workflow to automatically save documents from an
Envelope or Recipient event to a third-party storage provider or System of Record.

title: Store Envelope Files to Third-Party Storage from an Event
heading_path: Store Envelope Files to Third-Party Storage from an Event
text:
- Your workflow must use the From an Event start method.
- Configure the start method to use one of the Envelope or
  Recipient events.
- The extension app (for example, Google Drive, Box, SharePoint, OneDrive, or Salesforce) for your
  chosen storage provider or System of Record (SOR) is installed on your account. See [this help topic](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how administrators locate, install, and authorize third-party extension applications from the Docusign App Center to use inside Workflow Builder.") for more information
  about installing extension apps.

In a workflow that starts from an envelope event, you can use a Store
Files... step to capture documents from the envelope event and securely
archive them to your external cloud storage SOR provider. Using
