this feature ensures that your documents are automatically saved with the exact
configuration you need: individual files, a combined PDF, or a zip file.

Note:

The steps for this task show how to use this feature to store files to **Google
Drive**. You can use any of the extension apps that offer a
Store Files... step to archive documents to the storage
or SOR provider of your choice. The details for other apps may differ from what
is presented for the Google Drive app.

1. Edit your workflow and select Add a step >  Store files in Google Drive >  Configure.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/add_store_files_step.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MDEsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.T9QcPg7Kw_datKiyl2sY36aFx3_ieNC7RU_T9tO3eIU&_LANG=enus)

   The configuration panel for your Google store files step opens.
2. Select the Envelope Info from the list of Variables from Workflow Start as the source of the files you want to store:
   1. In the What are you storing?  field, select the
      dropdown and choose Variables from Workflow
      Start.
   2. Select Envelope Info.

      ![Screenshot showing initial configuration of the Store files step.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/envelope_info_variable.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MDEsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.T9QcPg7Kw_datKiyl2sY36aFx3_ieNC7RU_T9tO3eIU&_LANG=enus "Screenshot showing initial configuration of the Store files step.")
3. Choose how to configure the files you are storing by selecting one of the following options:
   - Combine all documents into a single PDF.
   - Store all documents individually.
   - Combine all documents into a ZIP file.

   (Optional) Check the Include Certificate of Completion box to store this file with your documents. ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/configure_store_files.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4MDEsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.T9QcPg7Kw_datKiyl2sY36aFx3_ieNC7RU_T9tO3eIU&_LANG=enus)
4. Select the location where the files will be stored, and click
   Next. Depending on which extension app and
   corresponding Store files... step you are using (for
   example, Google Drive, Salesforce, Box, and so on), the specifics for selecting
   the storage location will vary. For example,

   - For Google Drive, select your connection, drive, and folder.
   - For Salesforce, select your connection and a Record id variable.
5. Create a custom file name, and then select Apply:
   1. Use the Add Variable and Add Text buttons to build the file name using envelope data and manual entries.
   2. (Optional) Check the Add numbered suffix to files with duplicate titles box to ensure unique file names.

title: Store Envelope Files to Third-Party Storage from an Event
heading_path: Store Envelope Files to Third-Party Storage from an Event
text:
iable and Add Text buttons to build the file name using envelope data and manual entries.
   2. (Optional) Check the Add numbered suffix to files with duplicate titles box to ensure unique file names.

You have successfully configured the Store Files in Google Drive step
to save documents automatically from the specified envelope event to your third-party
storage location. This task used Google Drive as an example. You can store files to any
supported third-party storage location with the appropriate Store
files... extension app.

title: Create a Workflow from an eSignature Template
heading_path: Create a Workflow from an eSignature Template
text:
Learn how to generate a fully configured workflow directly from an existing eSignature template to quickly automate your signing process.

This topic explains how to create Workflow Builder workflows directly from your existing eSignature templates. This feature helps you easily turn simple document signing into automated, complex workflows.

As a process builder, you can start a workflow from various eSignature locations: from the main action menus of the Templates, Home, and Agreements pages, or from the Workflow Builder Workflows view. For more information on required permissions to complete this task, see [Workflow Builder Roles and Permission Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the different roles involved with creating and using workflows with Docusign Workflow Builder. Each role requires specific user permissions.").

When you create a workflow from an eSignature template, the system automatically configures key elements of your new workflow. You can use any eSignature template that meets the Workflow Builder template requirements. To review these requirements, see the help topic [Template Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=xnw1709852160602.html&_LANG=enus "Important information for process builders to consider before starting to develop workflows that include a Prepare eSignature Template step. When you prepare a template to use in a Send Documents for Signature step, there are several requirements and limitations that must be respected to configure the step successfully."). The workflow you create will have the following structure:

- The workflow's start method will be set to Manually in Docusign.
- Recipients from the eSignature template are automatically added to the workflow's list of participants.
- The workflow will include three main process steps: a Collect Data with Web Forms, Prepare eSignature Template, and Send Documents for Signature.
- A web form is automatically created to collect recipient information for the Prepare and Send steps.

1. In your Docusign account do one of the following:
   1. From the Templates page, locate the template you want to use, select the more actions menu, and choose Add Automation with Workflow Builder .
   2. From the Home page, select Start >  Workflows  > Create New Workflow > Start with eSignature template. Select the template you want to use and follow the prompts.
   3. From the Workflows view on the Agreements page, select Create Workflow > Start with eSignature template. Select the template you want to use and follow the prompts.

   The Preparing your workflow... message displays while your new workflow is created.
2. You can now publish the workflow as is, or continue to add more workflow steps to meet your process needs as described in the article [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus "Learn the basics of how to create an agreement workflow. Process creators and account administrators can create workflows to automate the agreement process.").

   ![Screenshot of a workflow created from a template.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/wf-from-template.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU2OTQsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.4tdkLeqjMHOweJWK31AK7BvSJltdF7ogAfE4aUH9-mQ&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of a workflow created from a template.")

title: Send a Copy of a Completed Agreement in a Workflow
heading_path: Send a Copy of a Completed Agreement in a Workflow
text: Learn how to configure a workflow to automatically send a copy of a completed agreement to a recipient who isn't a signer.

title: Send a Copy of a Completed Agreement in a Workflow
heading_path: Send a Copy of a Completed Agreement in a Workflow
text:
- **Permissions:** You must have Administrator or appropriate Process Builder permissions to create workflows.
- **Template Setup:** Template requirements vary by the document creation step type:
  - **Document Template:** Include a "Signer" recipient without assigned fields. Their role and receipt order will be designated during workflow configuration. See [Configure a Prepare Document Template Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=cra1749229692878.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to incorporate a document template into your process to generate customized agreements automatically using workflow variables.").
  - **eSignature Template:** Add the copy recipient using the 'Receives a Copy' or 'Needs to View' role. Position this recipient within the signing order to ensure they receive the document in the desired state, either completed or still in process. See [Configure a Prepare eSignature Template Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=tskd83cc960-fb4b-4534-9573-1041591501e2.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to incorporate an eSignature template into your process, prefill recipient fields using upstream variables, and route completed documents to a secure repository.").

Once an agreement is fully signed and completed, you'll often need to provide a copy to someone who wasn't part of the signing process. For example, for record-keeping or internal review.

To achieve this routing, first properly configure your template (eSignature or document template), and then use the template in the Send Documents for Signature step. Make sure to review the Template Setup listed for this task. With the right set up, you can ensure that the right person automatically gets their copy of the completed document.

1. In the workflow editor, add and configure one or more Prepare Document Template steps or a Prepare eSignature Template step. These steps produce the documents that you will send for signature.

   Important: The template you select must meet the prerequisites listed for this task.
2. Add the Send Documents for Signature step and select the documents you want to send.
3. Configure the step to confirm which workflow participant will perform each template role. Depending on which type of document you're sending, do one of the following:

   - **Generated document**:
     - Add the participant to the template recipient without assigned fields.
     - Specify what the recipient must do: In the drop-down menu, select either Needs to view or Receives a copy.
   - **Prepared eSignature template**: Assign the participant to the copy recipient role defined on the template.
4. Confirm the signing order:
   1. Ensure the Set signing order option is selected.
   2. Assign the copy recipient to the **last** position in the signing order to guarantee they receive the completed document.
5. Provide the required participant information using workflow variables or manual entry. (Name and Email. Phone Number for Text message or WhatsApp delivery.)
6. Click Apply to save the changes to the step configuration.
7. Complete and publish the workflow.

The workflow is configured to automatically email the completed agreement to the copy recipient.

title: Configure a Risk Assessment Step
text:
Learn how to configure the Risk Assessment step in a workflow.
This step gathers participant risk signals for dynamic orchestration, data-driven decisions,
and simplified compliance.

title: Configure a Risk Assessment Step
text:
To configure Risk Assessment steps, process builders must meet
the following prerequisites:

- They must be an account administrator or have Workflows:
  Create permissions.
- They must have Risk Assessment enabled on their
  account.
- They must ensure that the participants in the Risk
  Assessment step are in the United States

You’re a process builder who wants to add a Risk Assessment
step to your agreement process. You’re adding this step to evaluate participants' risk
profiles and define subsequent steps based on this risk.

1. Create a new workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus "Learn the basics of how to create an agreement workflow. Process creators and account administrators can create workflows to automate the agreement process.").
2. Select Add a step > Risk Assessment > Configure.

   The configuration pane for your step opens and displays the
   Configure risk assessment inputs section.
3. Select the inputs that you want to execute the risk assessment on.

   Note: the Docusign teams recommend using all inputs for more accuracy.
4. Select Next.

   The Configure the data to store section
   displays.
5. Select the data that you want to store in the ID Evidence file.

   For more information about how these proof files work, see the [ID Evidence in Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=kmm1749205469137.html&_LANG=enus "Read about ID Evidence integration with Workflow Builder and how it helps deliver evidence of the ID verification process.") page.
6. Select Next.

   The Which participant’s identity needs to be
   verified? section displays.
7. Specify the participant for this step.

   Use the dropdown list to select existing participants in your workflow. Alternatively, select Add Participant to add new participants.
8. **Optional:** Select Continue to map data fields to map variables to risk assessment inputs.

   Selected variables prefill the participant's personal information.
9. Select Apply.

Workflow Builder has configured your step. You can view details from a completed
Risk Assessment step when monitoring an existing
workflow. Read the [Monitor a Running Workflow Instance](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=vcp1698707326210.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to check the progress of the agreement instances you start from a workflow.")
topic for information on monitoring a workflow process.

title: Configure a Risk Assessment Step
text:
If you add a Risk Assessment step to your workflow, consider
including a Store files step after it to record the
verification details. Adding this step allows ID Evidence to store the risk
assessment data in a proof file within the designated archive folder. For more
information, read the [ID Evidence in Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=kmm1749205469137.html&_LANG=enus "Read about ID Evidence integration with Workflow Builder and how it helps deliver evidence of the ID verification process.") page.

After you add a Store files step, consider defining [Configure a Branching Rule](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=mnv1698169910928.html&_LANG=enus "You can create conditional rules in your agreement workflow. These rules validate a condition and branch the workflow based on whether the condition is met or not.") or other agreement actions based
on the participants’ risk profile.

title: Create a Workflow from an Agreement Rule
heading_path: Create a Workflow from an Agreement Rule
text: Learn how to generate a workflow from an Agreement Rule, using the rule's event as the automation trigger and its archive action as the initial workflow step.

title: Create a Workflow from an Agreement Rule
heading_path: Create a Workflow from an Agreement Rule
text:
- You must have administrator privileges to access Agreement Rules.
- Create a rule with an Archive to action. This rule must have the Powered by Workflow Builder tag to be used to create a workflow.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/powered_by_Maestro.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU3MDgsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.VJ_VPfzbpByfK9KUhagy7RIhkA0g-fw2z9nf6VTQyi4&_LANG=enus)

This topic explains how to create a workflow from an Agreement Rule.

Important: Only Archive to rules created **after** this feature was released are workflow-powered and available to use to create a workflow.

When you create a workflow from a rule, Workflow Builder uses the rule's event as the workflow trigger and its Archive to action as the initial workflow step.
After the workflow is created, you can open it in Workflow Builder to add more steps.

Activity logs for Workflow Builder-powered agreement rules are available from the Agreement Rules list. See the topic [Access Rule Logs for Agreement Rules](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=tsk1244004b-76be-4f59-b82e-08bbc5700972.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to access execution logs for agreement rules used to trigger a Workflow Builder process.") for details.

For more information on Agreement Rules, see the help topic [Create and Manage Agreement Rules](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=btt1594673504460.html&_LANG=enus).

1. In Admin > Agreement Actions  > Rules, locate the rule with the "Powered by Workflow Builder" badge.

   At this time, only new rules created with an Archive to action are available for this process.
2. Select Actions > Add Automation Steps.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/add_automation_step.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU3MDgsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.VJ_VPfzbpByfK9KUhagy7RIhkA0g-fw2z9nf6VTQyi4&_LANG=enus)

   A new workflow is created with a From an Event start method. The trigger event is configured with the details from the agreement rule. The trigger event is set to Envelope Completed and the conditions specified in the rule are applied. A file archive step matching the selected rule's action is added to the workflow. ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/workflow_created.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU3MDgsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.VJ_VPfzbpByfK9KUhagy7RIhkA0g-fw2z9nf6VTQyi4&_LANG=enus)
3. Add more steps to the workflow as needed to continue your business process after the archive step.

Your workflow is created from the workflow-powered agreement rule. Note that changes to the rule you used to create the workflow are not reflected in the workflow. The workflow uses a system-generated copy of the rule that cannot be modified. To make changes, edit your workflow details directly.

title: View Account Activity Log
text: Learn how administrators can access an activity log that provides a comprehensive view of all Workflow Builder activity for the account.

title: View Account Activity Log
text:
You must be an account administrator or a workflow administrator to view the account-level activity log.

Account and workflow administrators can access an account-level activity log. This log provides a
comprehensive view of all Workflow Builder activity. The log details all actions taken on workflow
definitions and all workflow instances across the account.

1. Log in to your Docusign account and go to Agreements >  Workflows.
2. From the Workflows list, select the more actions menu and choose View Activity Log.

   ![A screenshot of the Docusign Workflows list page, showing a table of active workflows and the open activity log menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/View_activity_log.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU5MjYsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.YGwV3TLFw6e2szV9ZmfhAmwHbfyd2RjF2FZu7YgaJZ4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Docusign Workflows list page, showing a table of active workflows and the open activity log menu.")
3. Review the Activity Log details:

   - **Filter by date**. Use the Date selection to filter the log by a date range.
   - **Order by column**. Select any column to order the log in ascending or descending order.
   - **Page through results**. Use the pagination controls to page through the listings.
   - **Download data**. Use the Download CSV option to generate a CSV file with your data for further processing and analysis.

   ![A screenshot of the Workflows Activity Log page, showing a table of recent workflow events with details like date, resource, action, and user.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/activity_log_example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU5MjYsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.YGwV3TLFw6e2szV9ZmfhAmwHbfyd2RjF2FZu7YgaJZ4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Workflows Activity Log page, showing a table of recent workflow events with details like date, resource, action, and user.")

title: Configure an Add Parallel Branches Step
heading_path: Configure an Add Parallel Branches Step
text:
Use the Add Parallel Branches step to split a single workflow path into multiple branches that run at the same time.

The **Add Parallel Branches** step creates a point in your workflow where the path splits into multiple branches that run concurrently. Use this step when you want approvals, reviews, or other tasks to happen in parallel instead of one after another.

Parallel branches provide the following support:

- Create up to 15 branches from a single point in the workflow.
- Define distinct paths for different teams or tasks, such as Legal, Procurement, and Finance.
- Reduce overall cycle time by allowing independent work to proceed in parallel.

Tip: Use **Add Parallel Branches** when multiple paths should run at the same time. Use branching rule steps when you want a single path to be chosen based on specific conditions.

The Add Parallel Branches step creates a point in your workflow where the path splits into multiple branches that run concurrently. Use this step when you want approvals, reviews, or other tasks to happen in parallel instead of one after another.

**Limitations for Parallel Branches**

- **Branch limit:** You can create a maximum of 15 branches within a single parallel step.
- **Plan availability:** This feature is available only for IAM Professional and above plans (including CX, Sales, Professional, and Enterprise).
- **Step limit:** You can add a maximum of 2 Add Parallel Branches steps per workflow.
- **Participant limit:** Only one participant can participate in a single branch of a parallel step.
- **Agreement Desk restrictions:** If the workflow starts with an Agreement Desk step followed by a parallel step, you cannot use the Change Status, Set Due Date, and Assign Owner steps in multiple parallel branches.
- **Retry restriction:** You cannot retry any step within a workflow if that workflow contains an Add Parallel Branches step.

**Before you begin**

- You have already created the workflow, as described in the basic workflow creation task.
- You have identified where the workflow should begin running steps in parallel. For example, after data collection or an initial document preparation step.

1. Open your workflow in edit mode and go to the workflow canvas.

   Locate the step after which the flow should split into parallel paths.
2. Add the Add Parallel Branches step.

   Select Add a step at the desired point in the workflow, choose Add Parallel Branches, and then select Edit.
3. Add additional branches as needed.

   Use the Add Branch control to create more parallel paths, up to a maximum of 15 branches for this step.
4. Apply the parallel branch configuration.

   When you finish adding branches, select Apply to save the configuration for the **Add Parallel Branches** step.
5. Configure the steps in each branch.

   For every branch, add the workflow steps you want to run in that path, such as sending documents for signature or calling third-party apps.

   Each branch can contain its own unique sequence of steps tailored to that team or task.![Example of an Add Parallel Branches step with 3 branches.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/example_parallel_branches.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4NTEsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.4hRA8h9WwHVirVbZ34ssSEFBdYos8RnIu0ECrjS5Q9U&_LANG=enus "Example of an Add Parallel Branches step with 3 branches.")

The workflow now includes a parallel section where multiple branches run at the same time. You can continue building the workflow after the parallel section by adding any additional steps required to complete your process.

title: View Workflow and Instance Activity Logs
heading_path: View Workflow and Instance Activity Logs
text:
Learn how process builders can view activity for their workflows, covering both actions on workflow definitions and all instances for these workflows.
Preparers can access the instance activity log to view activity for the instances they initiate.

title: View Workflow and Instance Activity Logs
heading_path: View Workflow and Instance Activity Logs
text:
You must be either a process builder or preparer as defined in the topic [Workflow Builder Roles and Permission Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the different roles involved with creating and using workflows with Docusign Workflow Builder. Each role requires specific user permissions."). Account and workflow administrators have access to all Workflow Builder activity log data.

Process builders and preparers can access activity logs specific to the workflows and instances they own.

- **Process builders**: can access a workflow activity log for the workflows they create. This log provides a detailed view of all activity for a given workflow. These details include all actions on the workflow definition and details of all instances for the workflow.
- **Preparers**: can access the instance activity log. This log provides a detailed record of all workflow steps for all instances initiated by the preparer.

1. Log in to your Docusign account and navigate to Agreements >  Workflows.
2. For **process builders**, from the Workflows list, select the more actions menu and choose View Activity Log.

   ![A screenshot of the Docusign Workflows list page, showing a table of active workflows and the open activity log menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/View_activity_log.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU5MjksInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.aYlctzmefs2U7MXz09ZuiGJ_AtR5hyv9YufrU5qVAPI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Docusign Workflows list page, showing a table of active workflows and the open activity log menu.")
3. For **preparers**, from the Workflows list:
   1. Locate the workflow you ran and select the more actions menu.
   2. Choose View Instances.
   3. On the Instances page, select the more actions menu for the specific instance and choose View Activity Log.

   ![A screenshot of a workflow's Instances page, showing an individual instance with its status and an open more actions menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/instance_activity_log.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU5MjksInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.aYlctzmefs2U7MXz09ZuiGJ_AtR5hyv9YufrU5qVAPI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a workflow's Instances page, showing an individual instance with its status and an open more actions menu.")
4. For either log, review the details:

   - **Filter by date**. Use the Date selection to narrow the log to a specific time range.
   - **Order by column**. Select any column to order the log in ascending or descending order.
   - **Page through results**. Use the pagination controls to navigate through long lists.
   - **Download data**. Use the Download CSV option to generate a file for further processing and analysis.

   ![A screenshot of the Workflows Activity Log page, showing a table of recent workflow events with details like date, resource, action, and user.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/activity_log_example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU5MjksInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.aYlctzmefs2U7MXz09ZuiGJ_AtR5hyv9YufrU5qVAPI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Workflows Activity Log page, showing a table of recent workflow events with details like date, resource, action, and user.")

title: Configure a Prepare eSignature Template Step
heading_path: Configure a Prepare eSignature Template Step
text: Learn how to incorporate an eSignature template into your process, prefill recipient fields using upstream variables, and route completed documents to a secure repository.

title: Configure a Prepare eSignature Template Step
heading_path: Configure a Prepare eSignature Template Step
text:
- Required: Access to a valid eSignature envelope template to use for your workflow step. For more information about template requirements, see the [Template Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=xnw1709852160602.html&_LANG=enus "Important information for process builders to consider before starting to develop workflows that include a Prepare eSignature Template step. When you prepare a template to use in a Send Documents for Signature step, there are several requirements and limitations that must be respected to configure the step successfully.") help topic.
- Recommended: If you are planning to map data from a web forms step, review the [Mapping Data From Web Forms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=cond11d1369-0918-4721-b25c-35149675b539.html&_LANG=enus "Learn the structural requirements and formatting rules for mapping advanced data types, such as checkboxes, radio groups, and dropdowns, from a web form to an agreement template.") help topic.

With the Prepare eSignature Template step, process builders can design a workflow to prepare a document to send for signature. This step uses an envelope template from your account. You can prefill recipient fields by mapping them to workflow variables.

A typical scenario for a Prepare eSignature Template step would be to start with a Collect Data with Web Forms step to gather participant information, such as name, email address, phone number and other data to be used in the document. Next, the Prepare eSignature Template step uses variables from the web form to prefill recipient fields in the document. A Send Documents for Signature step follows using the generated document.

![Screenshot of example workflow for a Prepare eSignature Template step.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/prepare-template-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU3MjcsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.YccPORBlmvU2yBTrQ0CIQQjuKtQvbyNl2y_nsibyG2k&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of example workflow for a Prepare eSignature Template step.")

1. Create a workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus "Learn the basics of how to create an agreement workflow. Process creators and account administrators can create workflows to automate the agreement process.").
2. Add the steps that you want to precede your Prepare eSignature Template step, such as a Collect Data with Web Forms step.
3. Select Add a step >  Prepare eSignature Template.
4. Select the template to use for the step and select Next.

   The selected template displays with the Mapping fields panel.
5. (Optional) Map workflow variables to template fields.

   Assigning workflow variables will prefill data in recipient fields. You can map any variable generated by steps occurring earlier in the workflow.

   Tip: Use the Map for Me option to generate recommended field mappings. The AI analyzes the **context of the document**—specifically the text surrounding a field and its physical placement—to suggest the best matches. Matches are based on this visual context rather than data labels or other field properties defined in your template. Review and apply the recommendations, or undo the action to use manual mapping.

   ![Assign workflow variables to template fields to prefill data for recipients.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/map_fields_env_template.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU3MjcsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.YccPORBlmvU2yBTrQ0CIQQjuKtQvbyNl2y_nsibyG2k&_LANG=enus "Assign workflow variables to template fields to prefill data for recipients.")
6. Select Next > Apply.

title: Configure a Prepare eSignature Template Step
heading_path: Configure a Prepare eSignature Template Step
text:
3MjcsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.YccPORBlmvU2yBTrQ0CIQQjuKtQvbyNl2y_nsibyG2k&_LANG=enus "Assign workflow variables to template fields to prefill data for recipients.")
6. Select Next > Apply.

Your Prepare eSignature Template step is configured. You can use the prepared template in a Send Documents for Signature step.

title: Generate a Screenshot of a Workflow
heading_path: Generate a Screenshot of a Workflow
text:
Learn how to create a comprehensive visual record of your workflow by generating a high-quality PNG image.

Workflows can be extensive and difficult to review in their entirety online. Use the screenshot option in the workflow canvas to generate a PNG image of your entire workflow. This feature helps you create a comprehensive visual record for:

- Creating visual references for documentation or training materials.
- Sharing workflow visuals with team members or stakeholders.
- Archiving workflow states for compliance or audit purposes.

1. Edit or Preview the workflow you want to screenshot.
2. In the workflow more actions menu, select Take a Screenshot.

   ![Screenshot of a workflow with the 'more actions' menu expanded, showing the 'Take a Screenshot' option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/take_screenshot.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4NzgsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.xphScrgnPO7_M2Tc0km3ZXGmMp_ADaeOR_DC8J5P-fI&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of a workflow with the 'more actions' menu expanded, showing the 'Take a Screenshot' option.")

   The system automatically generates and saves a PNG image of your complete workflow. Look for the file in your Downloads folder; it will use the workflow name as its file name. ![An example PNG image generated with the screenshot feature, displaying an entire workflow design.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/Example_screenshot.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4NzgsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.xphScrgnPO7_M2Tc0km3ZXGmMp_ADaeOR_DC8J5P-fI&_LANG=enus "An example PNG image generated with the screenshot feature, displaying an entire workflow design.")

title: Review a Migrated Workflow
text:
Learn how to review and publish a draft workflow after Docusign
automatically migrates it from an older format. When you publish the draft, that will
activate the new features and replace the active older version.

Once Docusign migrates your workflow draft, you must review the
draft for accuracy. As with any draft, when you're ready to use the updated workflow,
you must publish the changes.

1. Locate the workflow that you want to review, then select the more
   actions menu and choose Edit. If there is
   a saved draft of the workflow, this will continue to edit that draft.

   In the workflow designer, you should see the migration information banner.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/designer_migrated_workflow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4NzMsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.VpGhrJhY4Ql_ft5s_r3aGKn8mFzEAg9uvNtKhsSyyXU&_LANG=enus)
2. Review the migrated workflow to ensure that all steps, participant information,
   and conditional paths are correct.
   - The Get Signatures step has been separated into two steps: Prepare
     eSignature Template and Send Documents for Signature. Review the
     configuration settings for each step to confirm the template, mapped,
     fields, participant information, and so on, is correct.
   - Participant information that was previously in an Update Participant
     Information step is now automatically distributed to the relevant steps
     where it is used.
3. (Optional) Add any new features that you want to use, such as the Prepare
   Document Template step or merging paths of branching rules.
4. Once you have reviewed and finished updating the draft, select
   Publish Changes.

Your migrated workflow using the latest Workflow Builder features is published and ready to run.

title: Duplicate a Workflow
text:
Create an identical copy of an existing workflow to accelerate development and reuse standardized logic.

Duplicate an existing workflow to **create a complete, identical copy** and accelerate development. By reusing existing logic and configurations, you can build new processes without starting from scratch. This feature is restricted to workflow owners and administrators.

Important:

- The duplication is based on the **most recently saved version** (including unpublished drafts).
- Templates and web forms are **shared** between the original and the duplicate. They are not copied.
- The new workflow is saved automatically as an **Unpublished Draft**.

1. Browse to the **Workflows** list page or open a workflow in the builder canvas.
2. Access the **Duplicate** command through one of these methods:
   - **From the Workflows List:** Locate the workflow, select the **Menu (...)**, and click **Duplicate**.
   - **From the Builder Canvas:** Open the workflow, click the **Menu (...)** in the top-right corner of the canvas, and select **Duplicate**.![Workflow canvas with the actions menu expanded and the Duplicate option selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/duplicate_wf.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4NjgsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.WFSc-s3J5ouwQdFZCnNm3I8kL2jlLJpmBITfvquETYY&_LANG=enus "Workflow canvas with the actions menu expanded and the Duplicate option selected.")

   The workflow is duplicated and open for editing.
3. (Optional) Rename the duplicate workflow.

   "COPY" is appended to the beginning of the workflow name, and the status is "Draft (Unpublished)". Click the Menu (...) and select Rename.
4. Select Save Draft to save your duplicate workflow.

The workflow is duplicated and added to your Workflows list.

title: Verify Someone's Identity Step
text:
Learn about Verify Someone's Identity steps and how to use them to secure your automated agreement processes.

Process builders can use the Verify Someone's Identity step to verify a participant's identity. Depending on the step’s configuration, participants can use a government-issued ID, an electronic ID, or Knowledge-based authentication to pass the verification. They may also have to complete a biometric liveness check.

Unlike Send Documents for Signature steps, which offer built-in identity verification tied to an envelope, Verify Someone's Identity steps allow process builders to request a standalone identity verification in a workflow. This verification takes place independently of an envelope. For example, process builders can use this step to verify someone's identity when they open a bank account. As a general guideline, you can also use the Verify Someone's Identity step when there is no need for an eSignature envelope.

Verify Someone's Identity steps can generate an identity claim to store the participants' ID Verification results. Workflow Builder then applies the results from the identity claims to subsequent Send Documents for Signature steps. If a Send Documents for Signature step requires identity verification, the system uses the prior identity claim to confirm the participant's identity. This makes the experience smoother by preventing participants from going through the verification process twice in the same workflow. For more information about identity claims, read the [Identity Claims](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=vwg1731931235105.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about identity claims and how you can use them to streamline your automated agreement processes.").

Note: Knowledge-based authentication is only available for US citizens and US residents with valid social security numbers and credit history. In addition, signers must have public records to generate questions and answers. For more information, see the  [Knowledge-Based Authentication (KBA)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ced1643229641057&topicId=iiv1653399723856.html&_LANG=enus)  page.

title: Verify Someone's Identity Step
heading_path: Verify Someone's Identity and Send Documents for Signature steps
text:
Process builders can verify participants' identity in a workflow using the Verify Someone's Identity and Send Documents for Signature steps. The Verify Someone's Identity step is a standalone workflow step used in various situations. In contrast, process builders only enforce Send Documents for Signature steps when a recipient tries to access an envelope.

If your workflow only requires identity verification to protect your envelope, use the template in a Send Documents for Signature step. Instead, suppose you need to verify someone’s identity as part of a workflow step unrelated to an envelope. In that case, you can use the Verify Someone's Identity step - even if your workflow does not include a Send Documents for Signature step.

Process builders can combine the Verify Someone's Identity and Send Documents for Signature steps in the same workflow. The identity claim that results from the Verify Someone's Identity step can validate the identity verification configured within the Send Documents for Signature step. Using Identity claims, we help participants avoid performing the complete identity verification again.

Read the [Identity Claims](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=vwg1731931235105.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about identity claims and how you can use them to streamline your automated agreement processes.") page for more information about identity claims. Also, read the [Example of a Verify Someone's Identity step use case](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uak1735246809132.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=uak1735246809132__section_ykh_tdj_vdc) section for an example workflow. It includes both the Verify Someone's Identity and the Send Documents for Signature steps. It also uses identity claims generated in a Verify Someone's Identity step to skip the identity verification process in the Send Documents for Signature step.

title: Verify Someone's Identity Step
text:
When a process builder adds a Verify Someone's Identity step to a workflow, they must choose the identity verification configuration that will apply. Workflow Builder allows process builders to select only identity verification configurations that respect the following criteria:

- The configuration does not include the Manually review the ID document option
- The configuration does not include the Allow SMS authentication after one successful Photo ID or Passport verification option

Process builders can apply the identity claim from an earlier Verify Someone's Identity step to let participants skip the ID verification process in a Send Documents for Signature step. However, they must ensure both identity verification methods use the same configuration. Read the  [Identity Verification Configurations](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=eya1583277454804.html&_LANG=enus)  page for more information about the ID Verification configuration settings.

title: Verify Someone's Identity Step
text:
Process builders must collect participants' first and last names before adding a Verify Someone's Identity step to their workflow. They can collect participants' data in two ways:

- Using a Web Form where participants can enter their information.
- Using a connector to an external database that stores the participants' information.

Next, process builders use a Verify Someone’s Identity step to capture the participant's ID and extract its data depending on the identity verification method selected. It matches this data with the participant’s name from the Web Form or the external database. If the name matching and ID verification are successful, the ID Verification process generates an identity claim with the verification result for this participant. If the workflow includes a Send Documents for Signature step with identity verification, the system applies the identity claim to confirm the participant's identity. Thanks to the identity claim, the participant can skip the ID verification within the Send Documents for Signature step.

When process builders add a Verify Someone's Identity step to their workflow, they select one of the ID Verification configurations available to their account. The process for verifying participants’ identity depends on the ID Verification method included in each configuration. For example, suppose that a participant must comply with the "Knowledge-Based Authentication" configuration. In that case, they identify by answering questions based on their public records, such as their current home address. Knowledge-based authentication does not require ID capture and is only available to US residents.

title: Verify Someone's Identity Step
heading_path: Example of a Verify Someone's Identity step use case
text:
Imagine a workflow for opening a bank account. It could start with a Web Form step, where participants enter their personal information: name, email, and phone number. Next, in the Verify Someone's Identity step, they use their phone to capture a photo of the front and back of their ID. Depending on the ID Verification method, they may have to provide additional proof, such as completing a biometric liveness video.

ID Verification compares the name the participant provided through the web form with the name extracted from their government-issued photo ID. If both match, the participant can proceed to the next step in the workflow. The Verify Someone's Identity step also generates an identity claim with the verification result, the participant’s name, and the identity details.

The participant then enters a Send Documents for Signature step to sign the account opening agreement. The workflow requires them to verify their identity before accessing the agreement and supplemental documents. Since the ID Verification configuration in the Verify Someone's Identity step is the same as the one used in the Send Documents for Signature step’s template, Workflow Builder applies the participant's identity claim to skip the ID verification in the Send Documents for Signature step. It compares the name stored in the identity claim with the recipient's name in the envelope. If both match, the Send Documents for Signature step grants the participant access to the envelope. They can now sign the agreement and complete the bank account opening workflow. If the name stored in the identity claim does not match the name in the envelope, Workflow Builder cannot use the identity claim to skip the ID verification in the Send Documents for Signature step. The participant must verify their identity again to access the envelope.

title: Verify Someone's Identity Step
text:
Workflow Builder records data from Verify Someone's Identity steps to ensure transparency, accountability, and compliance with regulatory standards. You can access this information through any of the following ways:

- [The envelope’s Certificate of Completion](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uak1735246809132.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=uak1735246809132__section_z35_wdj_vdc)
- [The workflow details](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uak1735246809132.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=uak1735246809132__section_yw5_12j_vdc)
- [ID Evidence](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uak1735246809132.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=uak1735246809132__section_h2h_pvd_5fc)

title: Verify Someone's Identity Step
text: When a Send Documents for Signature step uses the identity claim from a Verify Someone's Identity step to confirm a participant's identity, the CoC includes verification data from the Verify Someone's Identity step. For more information about the CoC, read the  [Certificate of Completion](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=gpa1578456339545.html&_LANG=enus)  page. Read the [Identity Claims](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=vwg1731931235105.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about identity claims and how you can use them to streamline your automated agreement processes.") page for details on the requirements for using identity claims.

title: Verify Someone's Identity Step
text: When monitoring a running workflow, you can view details from a completed Verify Someone's Identity step. The Status Details for the step list key details for the Verify Someone's Identity activity. This information includes the identification configuration used, the date and time of the verification, and the result. Read the [Monitor a Running Workflow Instance](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=vcp1698707326210.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to check the progress of the agreement instances you start from a workflow.") topic for information on monitoring a workflow process.

title: Verify Someone's Identity Step
text: If the identity verification configuration used in a Verify Someone’s Identity step allows data storage, an ID Evidence file is generated when the identity verification is complete. This file includes all the stored data for identity verification, such as the name of the participants, the status of the verification, and a copy of the submitted ID document. For more information, read the [ID Evidence in Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=kmm1749205469137.html&_LANG=enus "Read about ID Evidence integration with Workflow Builder and how it helps deliver evidence of the ID verification process.") page.

title: Create a Basic Workflow
text:
Learn the basics of how to create an agreement workflow. Process creators and account administrators can create workflows to automate the agreement process.

Workflow Builder connects Docusign applications and third-party extension apps to automate your agreement processes and boost productivity. This topic covers the basics of creating a workflow for process creators and account administrators. More detailed information on specific workflow steps are presented in subsequent topics.

Before you begin
:   - Creating workflows requires [specific user permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the different roles involved with creating and using workflows with Docusign Workflow Builder. Each role requires specific user permissions."). Account administrators by default have all required permissions. For account users, if you cannot follow the instructions in this topic, check with your account administrator to review and adjust your user permissions. Review the topic [Workflow Builder Roles and Permission Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the different roles involved with creating and using workflows with Docusign Workflow Builder. Each role requires specific user permissions.").
    - How will your workflow be initiated? Manually by someone on your account? Or through an external system that will pass data into the process? Reference [Workflow Start Methods](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=ztb1727892686033.html&_LANG=enus "Review the different automated and manual trigger methods available to initiate processes in Workflow Builder, including links, events, API calls, and CLM integrations.").
    - Consider the roles that will participate in your agreement process. Who are the individuals and entities that will provide information, sign documents, and participate in any other way? In a workflow, these are called "participants". Review the [Define Workflow Participants](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=ypt1698264964168.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to define, configure, and manage participant roles within your workflow steps to control how users interact with your agreement automation.").
    - Workflows are built using preexisting web forms, eSignature templates, and document templates in your account. Before you create a workflow, you will need access to the web forms and templates you want to use in your agreement process.
    - For workflows that will be triggered through an external system that will pass data into the process, what data will be required at runtime? These are the starting variables you define for your workflow. Reference [General Limitations and Considerations for Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=otm1696972807664.html&_LANG=enus "Review important account limits, supported regions, and functional considerations before building automated processes in Workflow Builder."), "Data types and character limits for variables".
    - Your organization's account plan determines how many published and paused workflows you can have at a time. Once your account reaches this limit, you must unpublish a workflow before you can publish a new one. Reference [Unpublish or Delete a Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=kbn1698257312051.html&_LANG=enus "As a process builder, you can unpublish any of your paused workflows. You can edit and republish unpublished workflows, or you can permanently delete them.").

1. Log in to your Docusign account.
2. Select the Agreements tab and then select  Workflows in the left-hand navigation.
3. In the Workflows list, select Create Workflow.
4. Select + Blank Workflow > Continue.

title: Create a Basic Workflow
text:
to your Docusign account.
2. Select the Agreements tab and then select  Workflows in the left-hand navigation.
3. In the Workflows list, select Create Workflow.
4. Select + Blank Workflow > Continue.

   Your draft workflow displays on the canvas with the Add workflow start selection.

   Note: When creating a new workflow, you also have options to use one of our [pre-built workflow templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=irb1736981148403.html&_LANG=enus "Discover how pre-built templates in Workflow Builder provide ready-made blueprints to rapidly automate your business and agreement processes."), [create a workflow from one of your eSignature templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=tsk3aff5ded-8b1a-47e2-89a4-a6bca6c990be.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to generate a fully configured workflow directly from an existing eSignature template to quickly automate your signing process."), or [import a workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=zoi1742412999176.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to import a workflow into your account.").
5. Select Add workflow start, select the workflow start method that supports your business process.
   1. For API and CLM workflow starts, add variables for the data inputs coming from an external system.
   2. Select Apply.
6. Select the Manage workflow participants icon to add the participant roles for your process.

   Most steps require you to specify one or more participants to be used in the step. You can use any participant already configured, or you can add new ones through the step configuration. ![Screenshot of a draft workflow and indicating the Manage workflow participants icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/workflowsetupicon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU2ODAsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.SWkv8ly-9Q6HIirAz76NRfllSymWjesNQRmg4dYA8MA&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of a draft workflow and indicating the Manage workflow participants icon.")
7. (Optional) To control how emails and signing sessions look, select More (...) > Branding and choose a signing brand before publishing.

   See the article [Set Branding for a Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=task-6976.html&_LANG=enus "Choose a signing brand for your workflow to make sure all related emails and signing sessions use your custom logos and colors.") for details.
8. Select Add a step and then select the step type you want to add to your workflow.

   - To add a step between existing blocks, hover over the arrow on the canvas and click the +  icon.
   - To delete a step, select the step's three-dot action menu and choose Delete.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/add_new_step.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU2ODAsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.SWkv8ly-9Q6HIirAz76NRfllSymWjesNQRmg4dYA8MA&_LANG=enus)

   - The available step types depend on the applications and extensions authorized for your account.
   - The Add New Step panel includes a Home tab with all steps, and additional tabs that present steps in clear categories.
   - The Suggested next step section recommends the most relevant next actions based on your previous steps.
9. For each step you add, select Configure to configure the step.

   The configuration is different for each step type. Refer to the detailed step configuration topics for more information.
10. (Optional) Add a Path End step to all end points of your workflow.

title: Create a Basic Workflow
text:
tep.

   The configuration is different for each step type. Refer to the detailed step configuration topics for more information.
10. (Optional) Add a Path End step to all end points of your workflow.

    The Path End step is a visual indicator of final steps in a workflow. If you do not add these markers, they are added automatically when you publish a workflow. This utility step has no effect on workflow function; its purpose is to help you visually structure your design on the canvas. ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/path_end_example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU2ODAsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.SWkv8ly-9Q6HIirAz76NRfllSymWjesNQRmg4dYA8MA&_LANG=enus)
11. Once you've added and configured all your steps, your workflow will look something like this example, which uses all three Docusign apps (Web Forms, ID Verification, eSignature) and the Google file archive partner app:

    ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/sampleworkflow.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU2ODAsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.SWkv8ly-9Q6HIirAz76NRfllSymWjesNQRmg4dYA8MA&_LANG=enus)
12. Select the three-dot action menu at the top of the screen and select Save and Exit. Enter a name for your workflow draft and select Save & Exit.

    Note: You can edit your workflow name when you review and publish the workflow.

Your workflow is saved and appears in the Workflows list as an unpublished draft.

title: Create a Basic Workflow
text: [Review and Publish a Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=iqm1698272226447.html&_LANG=enus "As a process builder, publishing a workflow validates your steps are properly configured and makes the workflow available to your account members. Once published, preparers can start instances of your workflow.")

title: Cancel a Running Instance
text:
You can cancel any in progress instance of a workflow.

You can cancel any in progress instance of a workflow. Once an instance is complete or failed,
you can no longer cancel it.

You cannot restart an instance once you cancel it. Participants can still complete any
in-progress steps, but further steps in the workflow will not trigger.

1. From the Workflows list, select View Instances for a workflow to see the Instance
   list.
2. For any in progress instance, select Cancel.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/cancelrunninginstance.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU5MTYsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.62Rapy2SkTltHh0KZbWhwoHEeopqY8NIdKClCz1o9Bw&_LANG=enus)

The instance is canceled. Any in progress steps continue to
run, but no additional workflow steps will execute.

title: Configure a Send Documents for Signature Step
heading_path: Configure a Send Documents for Signature Step
text: Learn how to add and configure a workflow step to send a document for signature.

title: Configure a Send Documents for Signature Step
heading_path: Configure a Send Documents for Signature Step
text:
- Required: Workflows Create permissions or be an account administrator.
- Required: A Prepare eSignature Template or one or more Prepare Document Template steps prior to the Send Documents for Signature step in your workflow.
- Recommended: Open the template in another window to refer to as you set up your step. This will help you correctly configure your Send Documents step.
- Review the information in the topic [eSignature Requirements and Other Information](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=qkf1716338686555.html&_LANG=enus "Read about some important requirements to consider when configuring a Send Documents for Signature step.").

You use a Send Documents for Signature step to present a document to one or more participants to view, sign, or receive a copy. This step uses the output from either a Prepare eSignature Template or one or more Prepare Document Template steps. If your workflow contains multiple Prepare Document Template steps, you can send the generated documents together in one envelope.

For the signing session and all notification steps, Workflow Builder applies your account's default signing brand. This helps ensure brand consistency at key customer touchpoints.

1. Create a workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus "Learn the basics of how to create an agreement workflow. Process creators and account administrators can create workflows to automate the agreement process.").
2. Add the steps that you want to precede your Send Documents step. For example, a Set Up Invite step or a Collect Data with Web Forms step.
3. Add either a Prepare eSignature Template step or one or more Prepare Document Template steps. These steps produce the documents that you will send for signature.
4. Select Add a step >  Send Documents for Signature.
5. Select the documents that you want to send.
   1. Click the dropdown for the primary document and make a selection.
   2. (Optional) Select the Add Document dropdown and make a selection. If your workflow has multiple Prepare Document Template steps, you can add more generated documents.
   3. (Optional) Reorder the documents by using the grab handles to click and drag them into the desired order of presentation.

      Note: For additional information and limitations around adding and reordering documents, see [Managing Multiple Documents in Workflow Builder Envelopes: Order and Limits](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=con30310a57-3e53-40f2-a7f0-d9e099874eaa.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how to add, reorder, and combine multiple documents or templates in a Send Documents for Signature step, and learn about the limits for document types, quantity, and recipient mapping.")
   4. (Optional) Add supporting documents that are available in your workflow. Select Add Document > Add Supporting Document and select the document variable.

      These are ancillary files that do not contain any recipient roles or fields for data input. Examples of supporting documents include Salesforce files and Web Forms attachments.
   5. Select Next.

   Recipients for the templates you selected are added to the step
6. Choose how the send the envelope.

   For start methods that have a known initiator, Manually in Docusign and From an API call, you can choose between **Automatic** and **Manual** sending and on behalf of either the workflow publisher or the user who starts the workflow. See [Understand Manual and Automatic Sending and Sender Options](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=con50a84a0e-9d14-4028-98fa-e1b2d8405b48.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about manual and automatic sending options for envelopes in your workflows, including how to choose who appears as the sender.") for more information about these options.

title: Configure a Send Documents for Signature Step
heading_path: Configure a Send Documents for Signature Step
text:
405b48.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about manual and automatic sending options for envelopes in your workflows, including how to choose who appears as the sender.") for more information about these options.

   If your workflow uses the From an Agreement Desk Request start method, you can choose automatic sending on behalf of the Request submitter. See [Sender Impersonation and Consent for Agreement Desk Workflows](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map4317ef56-de36-43e2-9921-c76a445c3276&topicId=con85f610e6-2584-423e-840e-8823eb1ed065.html&_LANG=enus).
7. Confirm recipient details.
   1. Review and assign workflow participants to each template recipient role.
   2. Select the notification method (Email or Text message).
   3. Provide Name, Email, and Phone Number (for SMS/WhatsApp) using workflow variables or manual entry.

      Note: If the participant's information was defined in an earlier step, those details are applied here. You can keep the definitions or change them. Changes will apply to all subsequent steps.
8. Add envelope custom fields (ECFs).

   If your account uses these metadata fields for envelope classification, you must select values for any required ECFs. With text-type ECFs, you can use workflow variables to define values. ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/add-ecfs.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU3NzYsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.6YjiO3uQsXWa0JdQLUHmnOyYfo-UmR5dtUZer1wKaLE&_LANG=enus)
9. Select Apply.

Your Send Documents for Signature step is configured. When this step is triggered in a workflow, it creates an envelope in your account, accessible from your Agreements page. If you share access to your envelopes, these workflow-generated envelopes are included. A process preparer (the user who starts the workflow) who uses your workflow must have shared access to view the envelope. For information on sharing access to envelopes, see [this help topic](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=obf1692128351600.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Monitor a Running Workflow Instance
text:
Learn how to check the progress of the agreement instances you start from a workflow.

The workflow details views show you all agreement instances for the workflow. You can see all
in progress, completed, failed, and expired processes. You can see status details on each
step in the workflow and get information such as the completion time or reason for
failure.

Before you begin
:   - You must have Workflows: Prepare user permissions or greater.
    - Your account must have at least one workflow with one or more
      agreement instances.

1. Log in to your Docusign account and select Agreements >  Workflows.

   The Workflows list shows all of the workflows that you can
   access.
2. Locate the workflow that you want to use. Select the more actions
   menu and choose View Instances.

   The list of workflow instances displays.
3. Locate the instance you want to check. The PROGRESS
   column shows the state that the process is in.

   Tip: Use the Status
   filter to locate instances with errors you need to address. Or use the
   Search box to search for a specific instance name
   or ID.

   ![Screenshot of the instance list for a workflow with the Status filter expanded.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/maestroinstancefilters.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU5MTIsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.sDrmGta568SswF3KFVo0PSTpjCWbNdSJP4HnmJlZANY&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the instance list for a workflow with the Status filter expanded.")
4. For more information about the process, select the action
   menu and choose View details.

   The workflow canvas opens.
5. Select any workflow step and click View Details.

   - Details include step start and completion times and step participant
     role names.
   - For an in progress instance, the current step is outlined in black and labeled Active step.
   - Failed steps have a red outline and you can see what action to take.

   Note: If a failed step involves an extension app, check
   with your account administrator to see if the app is still installed and
   authorized for use.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/processdetails.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU5MTIsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.sDrmGta568SswF3KFVo0PSTpjCWbNdSJP4HnmJlZANY&_LANG=enus)

title: Managing Workflows
text:
Read about how
process
builders and administrators can publish and manage workflows.

As a
process
builder, you can edit, duplicate, or delete your saved workflow drafts. You can
also publish, pause, and unpublish your workflows. In this section, you will learn how
to accomplish all of these tasks and more.

Docusign and Workflow administrators can manage all workflows on an account. However, if a workflow includes a third-party app step that uses a private connection, the administrator cannot edit that step. Only the connection owner can edit a configuration that uses a private connection.

title: Identity Claims
text:
Learn about identity claims and how you can use them to streamline your automated agreement processes.

An identity claim is a set of verified information about a participant’s identity obtained
through a Verify Someone's Identity step. Workflow Builder uses this information to skip the identity verification process in a
Send Documents for Signature step of the same workflow instance.
When a Send Documents for Signature step confirms a participant's
identity using the identity claim from a Verify Someone's Identity
step, the Certificate of Completion (CoC) includes verification data from that step.

Note: Only certain ID Verification methods support identity claims. For more
information, read the [Supported identity verification methods](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=vwg1731931235105.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=vwg1731931235105__section_qjs_pyj_vdc)
section.

title: Identity Claims
text:
Whenever a participant verifies their identity in a Verify Someone's
Identity step using a supported method, Workflow Builder generates an
identity claim. The participants’ identity claim stores the ID verification results
and some of the participant’s information, like their name.

If the workflow has a Send Documents for Signature step requiring
additional identity verification using the same method that generated the identity
claim, participants do not need to verify their identity again. Instead, Workflow Builder applies
the identity claim to confirm their identity.

If a workflow includes several Verify Someone's Identity steps
for the same participant, Workflow Builder uses the matching identity claim with the most
recent validity period.

title: Identity Claims
text:
The Verify Someone's Identity step automatically generates an
identity claim. The Send Documents for Signature step
automatically uses this identity claim to skip the ID Verification provided you meet the
following requirements:

- The Verify Someone's Identity and the Send
  Documents for Signature steps belong to the same workflow
  instance
- The step for which you want to skip the ID verification process by using an identity
  claim is a Send Documents for Signature step with ID
  verification included within its template
- The ID verification configuration used in your Send Documents for
  Signature step is the same as the one used in the Verify
  Someone's Identity step. For example, imagine you added a
  Verify someone’s identity step to your workflow
  using a custom “Configuration XYZ” IDV configuration. Workflow Builder will use the ID Claim
  generated by that Verify someone’s identity step only if the
  ID verification configuration used in the Send Documents for
  Signature step is “Configuration XYZ.”
- The participant verifies their identity using a method that supports identity
  claims. For more information, read the [Supported identity verification methods](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=vwg1731931235105.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=vwg1731931235105__section_qjs_pyj_vdc)
  section. The participant uses the same device and browser to generate the identity
  claim in the Verify Someone's Identity step and use it in the
  Send Documents for Signature step. In other words, the
  participant goes through the workflow using the same device and browser for all its
  steps.
- The identity claim is still valid when reaching the workflow’s Send
  Documents for Signature step. For more information, read the [Supported identity verification methods](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=vwg1731931235105.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=vwg1731931235105__section_qjs_pyj_vdc) section.
- The name stored in the identity claim matches the recipient’s name in the envelope
  of the Send Documents for Signature step. If both names
  differ, the participant must verify their identity again to access the envelope.

title: Identity Claims
text:
Identity claims only accept identity verifications based on the following
methods:

- Photo ID
- Passport
- Liveness detection
- Knowledge-Based Authentication

Identity claims also have a validity period that differs depending on the applied
identity verification method.

If an identity claim expires, it cannot confirm the identity of its participant.
Therefore, Workflow Builder will not skip the identity verification in the Send
Documents for Signature step. It will require the participant to verify
their identity again.

If a workflow has several valid identity claims matching a Send Documents for
Signature ID Verification method for the same participant, Workflow Builder
applies the identity claim with the most recent validity period.

The following table describes the methods and corresponding signature types that
support identity claims. It also specifies the identity claims validity period
depending on the method used to generate them.

Table 1. Identity claims validity period

| Method | Signature Type | Identity claim validity period |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Knowledge-Based Authentication | Electronic | 24h |
| Photo ID or passport | Electronic | 24h |
| Photo ID or passport +Liveness detection | European Advanced Electronic Signature (AES) | 20 minutes |
| Photo ID or passport | European Advanced Electronic Signature (AES) | 20 minutes |
| Photo ID or Passport + Liveness detection | European Advanced Electronic Signature (AES) | 20 minutes |

title: Configure a Store Files in Google Drive Step
heading_path: Configure a Store Files in Google Drive Step
text: Learn how to configure a Google Drive storage step to automatically archive documents generated in earlier workflow steps.

title: Configure a Store Files in Google Drive Step
heading_path: Configure a Store Files in Google Drive Step
text:
- You must have [installed and authorized](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ctg1698170340729.html&_LANG=enus) the Google Drive extension app on your account.
- You must be an account administrator or have Workflows: Create user permissions as described in the [Workflow Builder Roles and Permission Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the different roles involved with creating and using workflows with Docusign Workflow Builder. Each role requires specific user permissions.") article.
- You must have a supported file type (Google Sheets, CSV, or MS Excel) saved on your configured Google Drive.

You can use the Store Files in Google Drive step to save documents generated by Send Documents for Signature or Verify Someone's Identity workflow steps. Generally, you will position your Google Drive archive step as follows depending on the use case:

- **Archive completed documents.** Add the Store Files... step at the end of your workflow to help ensure you are archiving a completed version of the document. Whatever state a document is in when the archive step generates is what is saved to the specified file location.
- **Archive an ID Evidence file**. If you want to access and export this file generated by a Verify Someone's Identity step, add the Store Files... step after the identity step.

Important:

Google Drive is one of several third-party storage apps supported for storing and archiving workflow files. For information on other file storing apps, see the [Configure Docusign Apps in Agreement Workflows](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=hno1706034267152.html&_LANG=enus) article.

1.
