Create a new workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus "Learn the basics of how to create an agreement workflow. Process creators and account administrators can create workflows to automate the agreement process.").
2. Select Add a step >  Store Files with Google Drive >  Configure.

   The configuration panel for your Google store files step opens.
3. Select the file to archive and select Next.

   Documents and proof files from any earlier Send Documents for Signature (Envelope) or Verify Someone's Identity (ID Evidence) steps are available to archive.
4. Specify where to archive the file:
   1. The default connection is the Google account configured by your account administrator.
   2. Select the drive. All available drives connected to the Google account display.
   3. (Optional) Select a folder on the selected drive. Listed are all existing folders and an option to create a new folder. For a new folder, you can create a folder name with text and any available workflow variables.

      ![Create a new folder for Google Drive using text.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/createnewgooglefolder.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU3OTgsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.HklpnyJcvJ3KqbRjO1x3Wl12MagzFXLBBHeWCX5zeQ8&_LANG=enus "Create a new folder for Google Drive using text.")
   4. Once you define the full path for the stored file, select Next.
5. Specify a file name for your archive. Use the text and variable controls to build your file name.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/filenamegooglearchive.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU3OTgsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.HklpnyJcvJ3KqbRjO1x3Wl12MagzFXLBBHeWCX5zeQ8&_LANG=enus)
6. Review the completed file path and select Apply.

Your Store Files in Google Drive step is configured.

title: Frequently Asked Questions About Identity Claims
heading_path: Frequently Asked Questions About Identity Claims
text: List of common questions and answers about identity claims in Workflow Builder.

title: Frequently Asked Questions About Identity Claims
heading_path: Frequently Asked Questions About Identity Claims
text:
An identity claim is a set of verified information about a participant’s identity
generated during a Verify Someone's Identity step. Once created, the identity claim can be applied later in a Send
Documents for Signature step of the same workflow instance, allowing
the system to skip identity re-verification for the participant.

title: Frequently Asked Questions About Identity Claims
heading_path: Frequently Asked Questions About Identity Claims
text:
Identity claims streamline the signing process by eliminating repeated identity checks.
If a participant verified their identity in a Verify Someone's
Identity step (using a supported method), the system will use
the existing identity claim to confirm their identity in the Send Documents
for Signature step. When a Send Documents for
Signature step confirms a participant's identity using the identity claim
from a Verify Someone's Identity step, the Certificate of
Completion (CoC) includes verification data from that step.

title: Frequently Asked Questions About Identity Claims
heading_path: Frequently Asked Questions About Identity Claims
How does Workflow Builder generate an identity claim?
text:
Workflow Builder generates an identity claim automatically when a participant verifies their
identity in a Verify Someone's Identity step using a method
that supports identity claims.

title: Frequently Asked Questions About Identity Claims
heading_path: Frequently Asked Questions About Identity Claims
Can I skip the Verify Someone's Identity step if I already have an identity claim?
text:
No. Only Send Documents for Signature steps can benefit from
identity claims. Verify Someone's Identity is the step that
generates identity claims.

title: Frequently Asked Questions About Identity Claims
heading_path: Frequently Asked Questions About Identity Claims
What happens if an identity claim expires?
text:
When an identity claim expires, it can no longer confirm the participant’s identity. The
participant must go through the identity verification process again in the
Send Documents for Signature step.

title: Frequently Asked Questions About Identity Claims
heading_path: Frequently Asked Questions About Identity Claims
Can I re-use an identity claim in different workflow instances?
text:
No. Identity claims are workflow-instance specific. They only apply to the same
instance in which they were generated.

title: Frequently Asked Questions About Identity Claims
heading_path: Frequently Asked Questions About Identity Claims
How do I know if my identity verification method is supported?
text: Check the [Supported identity verification methods](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=vwg1731931235105.html&anchor=vwg1731931235105__section_qjs_pyj_vdc&_LANG=enus) section.

title: Frequently Asked Questions About Identity Claims
heading_path: Frequently Asked Questions About Identity Claims
What happens if multiple valid identity claims meet the method requirement?
text:
Workflow Builder applies the newest valid identity claim that meets the verification requirements
in the Send Documents for Signature step. If multiple identity
claims match, the system always selects the one with the most recent validity period.

title: Frequently Asked Questions About Identity Claims
heading_path: Frequently Asked Questions About Identity Claims
Can an identity claim verify multiple participants?
text: No. Each identity claim is tied to one participant’s identity and verification data.

title: Frequently Asked Questions About Identity Claims
heading_path: Frequently Asked Questions About Identity Claims
Do identity claims create a Certificate of Completion (CoC) record for Verify Someone's Identity steps?
text:
The CoC record only appears if a Send Documents for Signature
step is part of the process. To record information from a Verify Someone's
Identity step in the CoC, your process must include a Send
Documents for Signature step. In addition, the Send Documents
for Signature step must use a template with ID Verification configured.
The Send Documents for Signature and the Verify
Someone's Identity steps must use the same identity verification
configuration.

title: Template Requirements
text:
Important information for process builders to consider before starting to develop
workflows that include a Prepare eSignature Template step. When you prepare a template to use in a Send Documents for Signature step, there are several requirements and limitations that must be respected to configure the step
successfully.

When you configure a Prepare eSignature Template workflow step, the template you select for the step is
subject to certain requirements and limitations. If your template does not meet these
requirements, or if it uses any of the unsupported features listed, your
step will not process successfully. Docusign will continue to develop
the Workflow Builder service, and potentially address some of these limitations in future
releases.

Note: In addition to these template requirements, you should also
review this topic [General Limitations and Considerations for Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=otm1696972807664.html&_LANG=enus "Review important account limits, supported regions, and functional considerations before building automated processes in Workflow Builder.").

title: Template Requirements
text:
When you configure a Prepare eSignature Template step in a workflow, you may encounter an error message:
This template is not supported. The error message lists the
unsupported conditions. Either edit your template to remove these features or select
a different template. You can find details about the unsupported features in the
following section.

title: Template Requirements
text:
To use a Prepare eSignature Template step in your workflow, you employ an eSignature template. Your
template must meet certain requirements. (These requirements are subject to change
in subsequent releases.)

Recipient types
:   Only the Needs to Sign, Receives a Copy, and Needs to View recipient types
    are supported.

Unique recipient roles and recipient names
:   The recipient roles and any named recipients must be unique values. Your
    template cannot have duplicate role or recipient names.

Recipients
:   Your template must have at least one configured recipient.

Signing groups
:   Signing group recipients are not supported.

Conditional routing
:   Routing group recipients are not supported.

ID Verification
:   ID verification specified on your template is supported. You can also add a separate
    workflow step for ID verification. If the template and the Verify
    Someone's Identity step use the same workflow method for the same
    participant name, the result (identity claim) is recorded on the eSignature
    Certificate of Completion. For details, see the topic [Identity Claims](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=vwg1731931235105.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about identity claims and how you can use them to streamline your automated agreement processes.").

Signature type
:   The following signature types are not supported:

    - ICP Smart Card
    - ICP-Brasil com Carimbo do Tempo
    - DS EU Advanced
    - DS EU Qualified with in person ID check
    - IDV - DS EU Advanced
    - IDV - DS EU Qualified

Delivery method
:   Workflow Builder supports all Docusign multi-channel delivery options for notifications: email, SMS,
    and WhatsApp.

Branding
:   When a template has a signing brand, that brand is used for all emails and signing sessions when you use the template in a Send Documents for Signature step.
:   If you choose a signing brand at the workflow level and opt to have it override the template brand, it can replace the template brand. The only exception to this override is when the template branding is locked. A template is brand-locked when an eSignature template owner or administrator turns off the **Allow senders to edit the logo and brand colors** option in the template's Advanced Options. In that case, the template's branding always applies and the workflow cannot change it.
:   See also: [Set Branding for a Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=task-6976.html&_LANG=enus "Choose a signing brand for your workflow to make sure all related emails and signing sessions use your custom logos and colors.")

Multi-recipient templates and direct signing
:   On a template with multiple recipients, only the first
    recipient can be routed directly to signing. Additional recipients follow the
    standard send for signature notification and routing process. See the topic [Configure a Send Documents for Signature Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uww1698167288211.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to add and configure a workflow step to send a document for signature.") for more information.

Envelope custom fields
:   Required envelope custom fields (ECFs) that are blank on your template must be mapped to a workflow variable or have a hardcoded value entered when configuring the subsequent Send Documents for Signature step. You cannot finish configuring or saving that sending step if a required ECF remains unmapped or empty.

    Your template can have optional ECFs that are blank or ECFs with hard-coded values. Blank
    ECFs will remain blank for envelopes sent through a Workflow Builder
    workflow.

Email subject
:   The template must have a message for recipients with an email subject line
    specified.

Field labels best practice
:   Adding descriptive field labels to your template fields helps process
    builders create accurate data connections.

title: Template Requirements
text:
ecipients with an email subject line
    specified.

Field labels best practice
:   Adding descriptive field labels to your template fields helps process
    builders create accurate data connections.

Field types for data mapping
:   You can map workflow data to the following field types:

    - Text
    - Note
    - Name
    - Email
    - Phone
    - Number
    - Checkbox
    - Radio button
    - Dropdown

    Other field types cannot be used for data mapping but are still valid
    for recipients to complete.

Pre-fill fields
:   Empty pre-fill fields are not supported. If your template contains pre-fill
    fields they must have values entered.

Merge fields
:   Merge fields, including document generation fields, are not supported. Only
    data gathered through the workflow (such as on a web form) can be mapped to
    a document text field.

Elastic templates are not supported
:   You cannot use an elastic template in a Prepare eSignature Template step.

title: Configure a Multi Branch Rule
text:
Learn how to use the Add a Multi Branch Rule step in Docusign Workflow Builder to create workflows with multiple paths based on specific conditions. This feature allows for dynamic processes that adapt based on data collected from web forms.

A multi branch rule creates a step where the workflow path diverges into up to 15 different paths based on a condition. It allows you to create workflows where the next step depends on the information collected earlier, such as selections made in a web form.

The Multi Branch Rule allows a workflow path to diverge into multiple different paths. For example, you can use it to send different documents to users based on their state of residence. Your rule would have a path for each possible state entered by the user.

Multi branch rules provide the following support:

- Define up to 15 conditional branches for a single rule evaluating a single workflow variable.
- Currently, it only supports an "equals" comparison. This means the value collected (for example, from a web form) must exactly match the value specified in the rule for a specific branch to be taken. Future enhancements may include additional comparison types. The comparison matching is case insensitive.
- Each rule has a No Conditions Met branch. This fallback branch ensures that your workflow has a valid path to proceed on if none of the branch conditions are met.
- Merge two or more branches to simplify your workflow design and eliminate the need to duplicate follow on steps.
- Workflow monitoring provides visibility into which branch was executed for each workflow instance. [Monitor a Running Workflow Instance](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=vcp1698707326210.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to check the progress of the agreement instances you start from a workflow.")

Tip: By comparison, the Branching Rule step described in [Configure a Branching Rule](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=mnv1698169910928.html&_LANG=enus "You can create conditional rules in your agreement workflow. These rules validate a condition and branch the workflow based on whether the condition is met or not.") offers a true/false branch with a broader selection of comparison types. This rule is useful for testing conditions using a variety of comparison types (such as 'greater than,' 'less than,' or 'contains').

1. Create a workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus "Learn the basics of how to create an agreement workflow. Process creators and account administrators can create workflows to automate the agreement process.").
2. Add a Collect Data with Web Forms step. The web form will collect the information you will use in the rule for your branching step. For example, a form might collect a user's name, email address, rental date, and the state where their license was issued.
3. Select Add a step >  Add a Multi Branch Rule after your Collect Data with Web Forms step.

   - If you are adding the step between existing steps, select which path to move the steps that come after the new rule. Then select Continue.
   - If you are adding the step to the end of your workflow, continue to [the next step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=xtg1749585007265.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=xtg1749585007265__step_w1040ab1b7b3b5_w1041ab1b7b3_w1042ab1b7_w1043ab1).![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/multibranchrule.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4NDgsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.YTf0Up-wGRiqE98cyevZFh4wGiqgQOjBRtJonVfb6DY&_LANG=enus)

title: Configure a Multi Branch Rule
text:
en=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4NDgsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.YTf0Up-wGRiqE98cyevZFh4wGiqgQOjBRtJonVfb6DY&_LANG=enus)

   The configuration panel for the branching rule opens. The rule starts with one configurable branch and a No Conditions Met branch.
4. Select the variable for the rule to check against and specify a value to compare to. Then select Done.

   Example: Add a multi branch rule after a Collect Data with Web Forms step to automatically determine which contract to send for signature.
   - Select the variable to evaluate. Select the web form step and the specific variable that you want to evaluate from that form.
   - Define Branch 1. Enter the specific value that you want to compare against the information the workflow participant will provide in the web form variable. For example, if the variable is "ContractType", you might enter "Standard" or "Premium" here. For matching, this value is case-insensitive.
   - (Optional) Name the branch. Enter a descriptive name for the branch to easily identify it on the workflow canvas.
5. (Optional) Select Add Branch +  to add branches to your rule. You can configure up to 15 branches.
6. (Optional) Choose if any branches should merge. Select at least two branches to merge.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/merge_branches_selection.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4NDgsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.YTf0Up-wGRiqE98cyevZFh4wGiqgQOjBRtJonVfb6DY&_LANG=enus)

   Important: Variables and files generated from steps inside of a merged branching rule will not be available to use in steps occurring after the merge. For more details, see [Merging branches](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=jaf1716918359288.html&anchor=jaf1716918359288__section-426&_LANG=enus).
7. Select Apply.

Your Multi Branch Rule step is configured.

title: Configure a Multi Branch Rule
text: **Add steps to each branch**: After creating the branches, add steps to each branch, such as Send Documents for Signature steps with different documents. Each branch can have its own unique set of workflow steps.

title: Status Rules for a Send Documents Step
heading_path: Status Rules for a Send Documents Step
text:
Read about how you can use branching rules to check the status of an envelope generated from a
prior workflow step.

If your workflow includes a Send Documents for Signature step, you should include
branching rules to account for certain scenarios that lead to unplanned envelope events.
By using branching rules to check the status of an envelope, you can control what
happens in the workflow.

There are three statuses that you can check for:

- declined
- voided
- completed

title: Status Rules for a Send Documents Step
heading_path: Status Rules for a Send Documents Step
text:
If any recipient declines to sign, the workflow will still move to the next step. To control
the workflow progress, add a branching rule after the Send Documents step to check
for the decline status.

Example rule details to stop a workflow if a participant declines to sign:

- Configure the rule to check the Send Documents step variable `Envelope
  Status`, with the operator `Equal to`, for the result
  `declined`.
- Add a confirmation step to the True branch. Configure it to show a
  custom message to the Send Documents step participant, such as "Sorry you
  declined to sign this document".
- Add a confirmation step to the False branch. Configure it
  to display a Tasks Complete message.
- Continue your workflow steps on the False branch of the
  rule. These additional steps define the workflow process after the envelope
  successfully completes. ![Screenshot of a workflow example of a branching rule for status = declined.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/exampledeclinedrule.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4NDMsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.54N0ALt11MFJgiKY2NGZ0v0E6dM0wMO6JcLjgC42IVY&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of a workflow example of a branching rule for status = declined.")

title: Status Rules for a Send Documents Step
heading_path: Status Rules for a Send Documents Step
Sender voids an in progress envelope
text:
If the sender voids an envelope, any in progress signer is redirected to a page explaining the
voided status. The workflow will continue to the next step unless you add a
branching rule to check if the envelope `Envelope Status` is
`Equal to` the result `voided`.

Continue your workflow steps on the False branch of the rule.
These additional steps define the workflow process after the envelope successfully
completes.

title: Status Rules for a Send Documents Step
heading_path: Status Rules for a Send Documents Step
text:
The Send Documents for Signature workflow step will continue to the next step when an
envelope reaches a terminal state (declined, voided, or completed). With a branching
rule, you can ensure that the workflow continues to the next step if and only if the
envelope reaches a terminal state of completed. This rule provides the appropriate
guardrails for subsequent steps, such as writing data from the envelope back to
Salesforce. These steps are executed only when an envelope completes.

To confirm a successful result, add a branching rule after the Send Documents step to check if
the envelope `Envelope Status` is `Equal to` the
result `completed`. Continue your workflow steps on the
True branch of the rule.

title: Connect Events for Workflows
text:
Read about how Docusign administrators can use Docusign Connect events to track certain workflow events.

When certain workflow events occur, Workflow Builder publishes Connect trigger events. You can
also use other Connect events to start a workflow.

title: Connect Events for Workflows
text:
Workflow Builder publishes the following Connect trigger events:

- workflow-started
- workflow-completed
- workflow-failed

These workflow instance events are available to use in a custom Connect
configuration. For example, you can use these Connect events to trigger a
notification or record data in another application.

These workflow events are documented in the [Connect Events](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=vob1727899215236&topicId=lry1608071169447.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: Connect Events for Workflows
heading_path: Use Connect events to start a workflow
text:
With the Event start method, a workflow starts when a
specified Connect event occurs. The workflow starts automatically without manual
oversight or intervention. Workflows can be started based on events occurring within
the same Docusign account.

Learn more about the Event start method in the help topic
[Workflow Start Methods](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=ztb1727892686033.html&_LANG=enus "Review the different automated and manual trigger methods available to initiate processes in Workflow Builder, including links, events, API calls, and CLM integrations.").

title: Build or Update a Web Form with Attachment Fields
heading_path: Build or Update a Web Form with Attachment Fields
text:
Read about how to build or update a Web Form with attachment fields or import them from a template that includes attachments.

You can create attachment fields directly on a form or import them from a template that includes them. After you import the attachment fields, recipients can upload documents only through the form, not through the template.

Note: The attachment feature is available for forms built with templates and [standalone forms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=pbx1716585514741.html&_LANG=enus). In standalone forms, attachment features are available only when the form is used in a Docusign Workflow Builder workflow. Your account must be enabled with Workflow Builder to see this option. For more information about using attachments in Docusign Workflow Builder, see the [Docusign Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=dnx1696972415150.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

Recipients can upload only one file per attachment field, so anticipate how many attachments they may need. For example, if you import an attachment field for "Lease Agreements" and users might have multiple agreements, add additional attachment fields to the web form. You can add up to 15 attachment fields per form.

To import a template with attachment fields, build the form, and the fields are imported automatically. To add attachment fields manually, see the [Importing Attachment Fields from a eSignature Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=oyz1722621351928.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: Define Workflow Participants
text:
Learn how to define, configure, and manage participant roles within your workflow steps to control how users interact with your agreement automation.

When you create a workflow, consider the roles that will participate in your agreement process
and any data that must be provided at the beginning of the process. Who are the
individuals and entities that will provide information, sign documents, and participate
in any other way? In a workflow, these are called "participants".

Most workflow steps require you to specify a participant. The step participant is the role that
will take some action to complete the workflow step. Certain steps require
specific participant information. For example, a participant assigned to a Send Documents for Signature step requires variables (or hardcoded values) for their name and email
address. You define this information in the workflow step by mapping variables from previous workflow steps.

title: Define Workflow Participants
text:
Participants are created for each workflow. When you create a new workflow, you can
configure all of your participants up front. Or you can configure participants as
you build your workflow and add steps.

When you create a workflow step that requires a participant assignment, you can
select from your list of configured participants or add a new one.

As you add participants, give them meaningful role names that describe the kind of
activities and responsibilities they will have in your workflow.

![Screenshot of the participants setup in the Participants Manager window.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/participantsworkflowsetup.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU2ODYsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.f4wlRCLjPUbBWMhyJ9FHk6GU1XNcmKDnmLrBlTaWHhw&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the participants setup in the Participants Manager window.")

title: Define Workflow Participants
text:
Assigning a participant to a step grants them access to that step and defines at what point
they can enter the workflow. Only participants assigned to a particular step will be
able to see and complete the steps.

There are two ways that a participant can enter a workflow:

- **Participants that enter the workflow directly.** For From a Link, Manually in Docusign, and API Call (human start) methods, the user who launches the
  process is directed to the first workflow step and assigned as the
  first participant by default. See the article [Workflow Start Methods](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=ztb1727892686033.html&_LANG=enus "Review the different automated and manual trigger methods available to initiate processes in Workflow Builder, including links, events, API calls, and CLM integrations.").
- **Participants are invited to the workflow.** Any other participants who need
  to enter the workflow must be invited. This invitation provides a URL specific
  to the steps that are assigned to them. For example, for a Link start type
  workflow, the person using the link enters the workflow directly. The second
  participant of the workflow is invited to the workflow so that they can access
  their steps. Or for the API Automated Process start, any participants that are
  part of the workflow, including the first workflow step, need to be invited so
  that they can access their workflow steps. See the topic [Configure a Set Up Invite Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=kfx1698167344720.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use a Set Up Invite step in your agreement workflow to invite participants to join the workflow.").

title: Define Workflow Participants
text:
Some steps like Send Documents for Signature, Verify Someone’s Identity, and Set Up Invite require
participants' information like Name, Email, and Phone. This information could be
coming from multiple sources.

Here are some examples of using the different workflow start methods:

- For the From a Link start method, the person that starts the workflow can provide their own
  information in a Web Form step.
- For the Manually in Docusign start method, the person who launches the process can provide
  participant information in a Collect Data with Web Forms step.
- For API and CLM start types, participant information can come from an external system
  as a payload. This information is represented as starting variables that can be used as
  placeholders to map to the participant information in subsequent workflow steps.

Once you add a step to your workflow that requires participant information, you provide the data directly in the step configuration. You can map
variables from prior steps or hardcode specific values. ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/specify-participant-information.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU2ODYsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.f4wlRCLjPUbBWMhyJ9FHk6GU1XNcmKDnmLrBlTaWHhw&_LANG=enus)

Once defined, participant information is
set at a global level for the workflow. Any steps that come later in the workflow
use the information defined in the step.

If you need to change a participant's information at some point in your workflow,
you can map new values in any step that uses the information. The revised participant information is used for all subsequent steps, unless revised again in another step.

Note: If your workflow generates an identity claim from a Verify
Someone's Identity step, this claim can be applied in a
Send for Signature step of the same workflow instance.
However, if you update or change the participant's name after the identity claim is
established, then it is no longer available to subsequent workflow steps.

Here's an example of how you might build a simple workflow
and define participant information. In this
example, the Client participant completes a web form to provide their name and email address.
You add a Prepare eSignature Template step to create a document. You finish your workflow with a Send Documents for Signature step for the Client and Manager participants. This last step maps variables from the web form to define the participants information. ![Screenshot of an example workflow.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/example-wf-simple.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU2ODYsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.f4wlRCLjPUbBWMhyJ9FHk6GU1XNcmKDnmLrBlTaWHhw&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of an example workflow.")

title: Define Workflow Participants
text:
Details for the information defined and required for participants are available in the
workflow Participant Manager. Check these details to see the
steps added for each
recipient and the workflow steps that require the definitions. ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/viewparticipantdetails.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU2ODYsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.f4wlRCLjPUbBWMhyJ9FHk6GU1XNcmKDnmLrBlTaWHhw&_LANG=enus)

title: Try Workflow Builder for Free in a Developer Demo Account
heading_path: Try Workflow Builder for Free in a Developer Demo Account
text:
Read about how you can try out Docusign Workflow Builder for free. Create a no-cost Demo account
to explore what Workflow Builder can do to automate your business processes.

Docusign offers no-cost, no obligation developer accounts so you can try out our solutions in
our Demo environment. Docusign Workflow Builder is included in these free accounts.

If you'd like to explore Workflow Builder, we invite you to create a developer demo
account. With this demo account, you can create workflows with both Docusign
apps, like eSignature, and extension apps from our partners.

Creating a developer demo account is easy. Visit the [Docusign Developer
Center](https://developers.docusign.com/) and complete the simple sign up form to create your free account.

![Screenshot of the option on the Developer Center to create an account.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/createdevaccount.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU2MjgsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.HPmIEsxcvch0VA9aWzd3iA1JVOb14nRM7rUVJ5KXrGM&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the option on the Developer Center to create an account.")

title: Using Workflows
text:
Read about how preparers can use published workflows to start workflow processes.

For published workflows, the start method defined for each workflow determines how the
agreement process is started. In this section, you will find information on how each
start method is used to initiate a workflow, and how to monitor agreement processes.

title: Using Workflows
text:
Here's a short video to help you understand how to use the agreement workflows
available on your account and manage your workflow instances.

title: Edit a Workflow
text:
Learn how process
builders and administrators can edit a workflow.

As a
process
builder, you can edit any of your published, paused, or
unpublished workflows. When you edit a published or paused workflow, the current
version remains in effect until you publish your new draft. You can save your edits
to complete and publish later.

Note: Your workflow must be published or unpublished in order to
publish the changes from an edited draft. You cannot publish changes for a paused
workflow.

Editing a workflow allows you to change everything in the configuration, such as:

- Add a new workflow step
- Modify an existing workflow step
- Adjust workflow participants and details
- Modify the workflow name

Docusign administrators and Workflow administrators (who have the **[Workflows: Manage](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the different roles involved with creating and using workflows with Docusign Workflow Builder. Each role requires specific user permissions.")** permission) can also edit any workflow on the account. For details on the restrictions and permissions when editing workflow steps that use Extension Applications (third-party connections), refer to [Access and Permissions for Extension App Connections](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=cond7058d74-97dd-4ec3-9a23-86978310f74b.html&_LANG=enus "Outlines the access levels and permissions required to edit or publish workflow steps that use private or shared extension application connections.").

1. Log in to your Docusign account and select Agreements >  Workflows.

   The Workflows list shows all of the workflows you
   can access.
2. Locate the workflow you want to edit. Filter by Created by:
   Me to get a list of your editable workflows.
3. Select the workflow more actions menu and choose
   Edit. If you have a saved draft of the workflow,
   this
   will continue to edit that draft.
4. Make the changes to your workflow and then do one of the following:

   - Save your draft to edit later. Select the workflow action
     menu and choose Save and Exit.
     The workflow list indicates the saved draft, which you can continue to
     edit later or publish the changes when you are ready.
   - Publish your edited
     published
     workflow. Select
     Publish Changes.
     Review and address any errors and publish. Note that publishing
     allows you to edit the workflow name as part of the review process.

     Note: Your workflow must be published or
     unpublished in order to publish the changes from an edited draft.
     You cannot publish changes for a paused workflow.
   - Resume a paused workflow and publish. Select Save
     Draft. Next, select the workflow action
     menu and choose Resume New
     Instances. This returns your workflow to a published
     state and you can publish your changes.

title: Import a Workflow
text: Learn how to import a workflow into your account.

title: Import a Workflow
text:
- You must have Workflows: Create permissions to import
  workflows.
- If the workflow includes web form or eSignature steps, you'll need the
  permissions to create those assets as well.
- If the workflow includes any third-party extension app steps, those apps must be
  installed and connected before you import the workflow. Extension apps are
  managed by your account administrator.
- Have the ZIP file with the exported workflow available to import.

You can import an exported workflow into your account. You can also clone a workflow
by exporting it and then importing it to the same account.

1. Log in to your Docusign account and go to Agreements >  Workflows.
2. Select Create Workflow.

   ![Screenshot of the Create a workflow view and the Import Workflow option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yff1696971835267/page/images/importworkflowbutton.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDU4OTQsInNoZWFmIjoieWZmMTY5Njk3MTgzNTI2NyJ9.1W5SlsnZCXLlnqQh2uzAyCWjHEvQCnB8ibIzp703lh0&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the Create a workflow view and the Import Workflow option.")

   The Create a workflow view opens.
3. Select the Import Workflow tile.
4. In your file picker, open the zipped file containing the workflow export.

   The import process begins. The import may take a few minutes. Do not
   navigate away from the process until it finishes. When the import process is
   complete, your new workflow opens in the editing view.
5. Review each step in the workflow. Confirm or update step configurations as
   needed.
6. Publish the workflow or save the draft.

The workflow is imported into your account.

title: Workflow Start Methods
text:
Review the different automated and manual trigger methods available to initiate processes in Workflow Builder, including links, events, API calls, and CLM integrations.

Start methods define how a workflow will be initiated. Each method supports different use case scenarios. Select the method that best supports the way you want to use the workflow for your business process.

These start methods, including information about updating existing workflows to use a start method, are described in the following sections.

title: Workflow Start Methods
text:
Older published workflows that do not have a start method defined are not impacted by the new workflow start method. Any running instances for these workflows will continue, uninterrupted. However, to update an existing workflow definition (or when creating a new workflow), process builders must pick a workflow start method. You cannot publish edits to an existing workflow until you add a workflow start method.

For information on how to edit your existing workflow to use a start method, see the help topic [Update an Existing Workflow to Use a Start Method](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bqq1729024082952.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to update an older, published workflow by defining a required start method before saving and publishing new edits.").

title: Workflow Start Methods
text:
With the Link start method, a workflow starts when a participant opens a link. That link could be shared directly with the participant, or embedded on a website or in an application.

When should I use this start method?
:   Link starts are beneficial for self-service workflows like embedding the link in a customer portal or loan application site. In these types of scenarios, participants can initiate workflows on demand.

How can I use it?
:   Once published, a workflow with a link start has a URL. Use the generated URL to embed the workflow in a customer portal or share it directly with users. You can copy this URL from the Workflows list page.

    Note: The individual who clicks the link automatically becomes the first participant in that active instance.

title: Workflow Start Methods
text:
With the Manual start method, a workflow starts when someone decides to launch it and runs the workflow from the Workflows list.

When should I use this start method?
:   Manual starts are useful when whoever starts the workflow from within Docusign must specify who will be the workflow participants. They can input information like names and emails before inviting participants.

How can I use it?
:   The first participant must be the internal user launching the process manually in Docusign.

    Add a Collect Data with Web Forms as the first workflow step. Prepare and select a web form with fields for each piece of data the person starting the workflow must provide. Assign the web form step to the participant who'll start the workflow.

    Add a confirmation screen letting the participant know that’s the end of their tasks.

    To start an instance manually, select Run Workflow option from the Workflows list more actions menu.

title: Workflow Start Methods
text:
With the Event start method, a workflow starts when a specified event occurs. The workflow starts automatically without manual oversight or intervention. Workflows can be started based on events occurring within the same Docusign account.

Note: To use the From an Event or Agreement Desk request start methods, [Docusign Connect](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=vob1727899215236&topicId=ctv1583277395112.html&_LANG=enus) must be enabled on your account. If Connect is not enabled, you will receive a validation error when you attempt to publish the workflow.

When should I use this start method?
:   Event starts are useful when you want a workflow to start automatically when a specific event occurs.

How can I use it?
:   A process builder builds a workflow using the From an Event start method. The builder configures the start method with the event type that will trigger the workflow. Optionally, the builder specifies conditions for the selected event that must be met for the workflow to start.

    Supported events:

    - Envelope events
      - Envelope Completed
      - Envelope Voided
      - Envelope Declined
    - Recipient events
      - Recipient Authentication Failed
      - Recipient Automatically Responded
      - Recipient Declined
      - Recipient Sent
      - Recipient Completed
    - Workflow events
      - Workflow Completed
      - Workflow Failed

    Once the workflow is published, anytime the selected event occurs and any specified conditions are met, a workflow instance will start automatically.

title: Workflow Start Methods
text:
With the CLM workflow start method, a workflow starts when the associated CLM workflow reaches a specified step and launches Workflow Builder. At the end of the workflow, the process passes metadata back to CLM.

Note: This start method is available only for accounts that also have Docusign CLM enabled.

When should I use this start method?
:   Use this method when you want to combine CLM and Workflow Builder capabilities into a single automated process.

How can I use it?
:   Process builders can construct a workflow designed to be triggered directly from an active Docusign CLM process (both configurations must exist under the same organization account).

    Define starting variables for the CLM workflow start method. These are placeholders that define how information from the CLM workflow gets used in Workflow Builder. You will map these variables to properties in the workflow steps that manage communication between Workflow Builder and CLM.

    After you publish the workflow, create a CLM workflow with a corresponding step to trigger it.

    Reference: [Enhance CLM Workflows with Workflow Builder Automation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=hmz1727408977389.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how process builders trigger Workflow Builder processes from Docusign CLM to access advanced delivery options and signature features.")

Test the workflow
:   To test this workflow without running an active CLM process, select Run Workflow from the Workflows list more actions menu. Input dummy data for the starting variables.

title: Workflow Start Methods
text:
With the Agreement Desk request start method, a workflow starts when the specified request event occurs. The workflow executes the defined steps to automate key steps to triage and manage your Agreement Desk requests. With Workflow Builder, you can create workflows to manage incoming requests, create approvals, share and assign ownership, and even generate documents to add to a request.

Note: To use the From an Event or Agreement Desk request start methods, [Docusign Connect](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=vob1727899215236&topicId=ctv1583277395112.html&_LANG=enus) must be enabled on your account. If Connect is not enabled, you will receive a validation error when you attempt to publish the workflow.

When should I use this start method?
:   Use this method when you want to automate some of the manual tasks in managing Agreement Desk requests.

How can I use it?
:   A process builder creates a workflow with this start method and defines the event and conditions that will trigger the workflow. They add the steps desired to automate request actions and publish the workflow.

    Note: The Create Request step is not supported for workflows using this start method. If you want to generate a new Agreement Desk request, your workflow must use an alternative start method such as From a Link, From an API Call, From an Event, or Manually in Docusign.

Reference information
:   For an overview of how to use this start method, see [Automate Agreement Desk Requests with Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=tsk2231627c-1c61-4c10-b538-43bdc7021507.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to streamline the agreement lifecycle by using Workflow Builder to triage, manage, and automate incoming Agreement Desk requests.").
:   For detailed guidance, see [Automating Agreement Desk Requests with Docusign Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map4317ef56-de36-43e2-9921-c76a445c3276&topicId=con27b1a75a-da95-4952-94a4-a20c498bf81c.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Workflow Start Methods
text:
With the API call start method, a workflow starts after an API call is made to trigger it.

When should I use this start method?
:   Use this start when you want to launch the workflow from an external system. This can be either a participant taking a manual action or automatically triggered when a condition is met.

How can I use it?
:   Add variables to specify the information to send from an external system to your workflow. These variables represent dynamic inputs sent as a payload coming from the external system.

    For example, your customer starts a workflow from your website to submit an application. They've logged in to their account, so when they start the application, the system passes their name, contact information, and account number into the process. Map the variables to fields in workflow steps.

    **Reference**: "Data types and character limits for variables" in [Data Types for Workflow Variables](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=ogx1738026109020.html&_LANG=enus "Look up details about the data types that you can use to pass data to workflow variables.")

    Select how the API call will be initiated:

    - Human. Select this option if you intend this workflow to be started by a participant and redirected to the first step of the workflow, like to a Collect Data with Web Forms or Send Documents for Signature step. By default, the system assigns the individual who launched the external action as the first participant.
    - Automated process. Select the Automated Process option if you intend this workflow to be initiated by another system. If there are any participants in this workflow, you must invite them using a Set up Invite step.

    Once published, the workflow has a URL. Use the URL to configure the API call in the external system. You can copy this URL from the Workflows list.

    **API Reference**: [How to trigger a Workflow Builder workflow](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/maestro-api/how-to/trigger-workflow/)

Test the workflow
:   To test this workflow without having to make an API call, select Run Workflow from the Workflows list more actions menu. Enter dummy data for the created variables. Complete the steps if this is a Human-initiated workflow.
